{"id":"feedreader-47z","title":"News Alerts","description":"Add a news alerts feature: if an article matches a user-defined keyword or regex, it gets added to a news alerts list. Alerts are global scope, match on title+summary at ingest time, and have their own dismissed/acknowledged state separate from read/unread. Articles matching both an exclusion rule and an alert still appear in the alerts list.","design":"## Data Model
- `news_alerts` table: id, user_id, name, pattern, is_regex, match_field (default 'title_summary'), created_at
- `article_alerts` join table: article_id, alert_id, matched_at, dismissed (bool)
- Unique constraint on (article_id, alert_id)

## Matching Engine
- New `srv/alerts.go` — loads user alert rules, pre-compiles regexes, matches title+summary
- Hooked into `OnFeedFetched` callback alongside existing `MarkExcludedArticlesReadForFeed`
- Alert evaluation runs independently of exclusion logic (alert wins if both match)
- No retroactive backfill — only newly fetched articles evaluated

## API
- CRUD on `/api/alerts` + dismiss endpoints on `/api/alerts/{id}/dismiss` and `/api/article-alerts/{id}/dismiss`
- Alert count added to `/api/counts` response

## UI
- Alerts list page (`GET /alerts`) grouped by rule, similar to history page
- Per-alert detail page (`GET /alerts/{id}`) for editing rule + viewing matches
- Create button on alerts list page
- Sidebar badge for undismissed alert count

## JS
- New `srv/static/modules/alerts.js` + companion test file","acceptance_criteria":"1. Users can create keyword and regex alert rules
2. New articles matching alert rules are automatically added to the alerts list at ingest time
3. Alerts page shows matched articles grouped by rule
4. Per-alert page allows editing/deleting rules and dismissing matches
5. Sidebar shows undismissed alert count badge
6. Articles matching both exclusion rules and alerts still appear in alerts
7. All new code passes make check
8. JS module has companion test file","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:43:08Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-02T04:43:08Z"}

- `EvaluateAlertsForFeed(ctx, feedID)` — looks up feed owner, loads user's alert rules, pre-compiles regex patterns, fetches unread articles for the feed, matches each against title+summary, inserts into article_alerts on match
- Pattern matching: plain keyword = case-insensitive substring match on title and summary; regex = compiled regexp match
- Pre-compile regexes once per evaluation batch (same pattern as shouldExclude in filter.go)
- Log matched alerts at info level for observability
- Handle errors gracefully (skip bad regexes, log warnings)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:43:31Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-02T04:43:31Z"}

- POST /api/alerts — create alert (name, pattern, is_regex)

{"id":"feedreader-6ki","title":"Read articles hidden even with 'show all' enabled on feed pages","description":"## Bug
When viewing a feed (e.g., feed/586 — Hacker News: Best, 412 articles all read), read articles don't appear even when using 'show all articles' or search.

## Root Cause
Two interacting issues:

1. **showHiddenArticles re-hides what it just fetched**: `showHiddenArticles()` sets `showingHiddenArticles = true`, then calls `renderArticles()`, which calls `applyUserPreferences()` → `applyHideReadArticles('hide')`. This re-hides all read articles immediately after rendering them. The `showingHiddenArticles` flag is never checked by `applyHideReadArticles`.

2. **Search results also hidden**: `search.js` calls `renderArticles()` for search results, which also triggers `applyUserPreferences()`, hiding any read articles in the results. Search should show all matching articles regardless of the hideRead setting.

## Fix
`applyUserPreferences()` (or `applyHideReadArticles()`) should check `getShowingHiddenArticles()` and skip hiding when the user has explicitly asked to see all articles. Similarly, search results should bypass the hideRead filter.

## Affected files
- `srv/static/modules/articles.js` — `applyUserPreferences`, `showHiddenArticles`, `renderArticles`
- `srv/static/modules/settings.js` — `applyHideReadArticles`
- `srv/static/modules/search.js` — search rendering","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:00:16Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:00:16Z"}

This could be done server-side during rendering (sanitization/template) or client-side after render.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:18:15Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:18:15Z"}

{"notes":"## feedreader-cnt.1 & cnt.2: feeds.test.js and folders.test.js console.error assertions
Straightforward — the api() helper throws Error(message) where message comes from the JSON error field. Each catch block logs console.error with a prefix string + the error. Added toHaveBeenCalledWith('prefix:', expect.any(Error)) assertions alongside the existing showToast assertions.
## feedreader-cnt.3 & cnt.4: Remove blanket console.debug suppression
Simple removal — both files had vi.spyOn(console, 'debug').mockImplementation(() => {}) in the top-level beforeEach. All tests pass without it. The debug output comes from auto-mark-read logging in article-actions.js (observing/flushing messages). Tests that don't exercise auto-mark-read produce minimal noise (just 'disabled by setting'). No test environment configuration changes needed.
## cnt.3 & cnt.4 (amended): targeted mocks with assertions
Initial approach of just removing the blanket mock left noisy console.debug output in test runs. Corrected to: keep the mock but move it to targeted describe/test blocks with assertions. Key mapping:
- initAutoMarkRead → 'disabled by setting' or 'observing N initial articles'
- observeNewArticles → 'observing N new articles'
- markReadSilent/flushMarkReadQueue → 'flushing batch of N article(s):' with array arg
- renderArticles with empty/null → returns early before initAutoMarkRead, so assert not-called
- showHiddenArticles with no autoMarkRead setting → 'disabled by setting'"}

{"notes":"## feedreader-cnt.1 & cnt.2: feeds.test.js and folders.test.js console.error assertions
Straightforward — the api() helper throws Error(message) where message comes from the JSON error field. Each catch block logs console.error with a prefix string + the error. Added toHaveBeenCalledWith('prefix:', expect.any(Error)) assertions alongside the existing showToast assertions.
## feedreader-cnt.3 & cnt.4: Remove blanket console.debug suppression
Simple removal — both files had vi.spyOn(console, 'debug').mockImplementation(() => {}) in the top-level beforeEach. All tests pass without it. The debug output comes from auto-mark-read logging in article-actions.js (observing/flushing messages). Tests that don't exercise auto-mark-read produce minimal noise (just 'disabled by setting'). No test environment configuration changes needed.
## cnt.3 & cnt.4 (amended): targeted mocks with assertions
Initial approach of just removing the blanket mock left noisy console.debug output in test runs. Corrected to: keep the mock but move it to targeted describe/test blocks with assertions. Key mapping:
- initAutoMarkRead → 'disabled by setting' or 'observing N initial articles'
- observeNewArticles → 'observing N new articles'
- markReadSilent/flushMarkReadQueue → 'flushing batch of N article(s):' with array arg
- renderArticles with empty/null → returns early before initAutoMarkRead, so assert not-called
- showHiddenArticles with no autoMarkRead setting → 'disabled by setting'
## feedreader-cnt.5: pagination.test.js console.debug suppression
Simplest case — no console.debug calls fire in any pagination test because autoMarkReadObserver is null (never initialized in test environment), so observeNewArticles() returns early before logging. Blanket mock was purely precautionary. Just removed it."}

{"id":"feedreader-g2q.1","title":"Add error-path tests for markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue","description":"All four core action functions have catch blocks that call console.error and showToast, but none are tested on the error path.

Test cases needed (4 tests):
- markRead with fetch rejection: asserts console.error called with 'Failed to mark read:', showToast called with 'Failed to mark as read'
- markUnread with fetch rejection: asserts console.error and showToast, card retains .read class
- toggleStar with fetch rejection: asserts console.error and showToast
- toggleQueue with fetch rejection: asserts console.error and showToast, queuedArticleIds unchanged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:37Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:37Z"}

{"id":"feedreader-g2q.2","title":"Strengthen DOM mutation assertions for toggleStar, toggleQueue, and markRead","description":"The existing happy-path tests for toggleStar, toggleQueue, and markRead only assert that fetch was called. The functions also perform significant DOM mutations that go unverified.

Test cases needed:
- toggleStar: set up a button with data-action='toggle-star' and data-article-id, verify after call: .starred class toggled, title and aria-label updated, innerHTML switched between SVG_STAR_FILLED/SVG_STAR_EMPTY, updateCounts called
- toggleQueue: set up a queue button, verify after call: .queued class toggled, title/aria-label updated, innerHTML switched, queuedArticleIds updated (add on queued:true, delete on queued:false), updateCounts and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone called
- markRead: verify card gets .read class and updateReadButton called with (card, true)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:45Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:45Z"}

new_value: Added 4 error-path tests: markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue. Each verifies console.error message and showToast call on API failure. markUnread error test also verifies .read class retained.

Strengthened DOM mutation assertions: markRead verifies .read class + updateReadButton(card, true) + updateCounts. markUnread verifies updateReadButton(card, false) + updateCounts. toggleStar verifies class/title/aria-label/innerHTML in both star/unstar directions. toggleQueue verifies class/title/aria-label/innerHTML, queuedArticleIds add/delete, updateCounts, updateQueueCacheIfStandalone.

{"notes":"## g2q.1 + g2q.2: Error paths and DOM mutations
- JSDOM normalizes self-closing SVG tags (<path .../> → <path ...></path>), so innerHTML assertions must use toContain with distinguishing path data substrings rather than exact SVG constant matching.
- toast.js mock was added (vi.mock('./toast.js')) to assert showToast calls in error paths.
- The api() wrapper in api.js throws on !res.ok, so error path tests mock fetch to return { ok: false, status: 500, text: ... } which triggers the catch blocks.
- updateCounts, updateQueueCacheIfStandalone, and updateReadButton were already mocked; just needed to import them in the test file to assert they were called."}

{"id":"feedreader-g2q.3","title":"Add missing markAsRead test paths: feed, global, reload fallback, error","description":"markAsRead currently has one test covering only the category path. Four paths are untested:

1. **Feed path** — dropdown has data-feed-id set: should POST to /api/feeds/{id}/read-all?age={age}, then location.reload()
2. **Global path** — dropdown has neither feed nor category id: should POST to /api/articles/read-all?age={age}, then location.reload()
3. **Reload fallback** — category path but findNextUnreadFolder returns null (no other unread folders): should call location.reload() instead of navigating
4. **Error path** — API call fails: should call console.error and showToast
5. **Dropdown close** — after successful call, .dropdown.open elements should have .open removed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:53Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:53Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feedreader-g2q.4","title":"Fix openArticle test to assert navigation target","description":"The openArticle test mocks window.location and verifies fetch was called, but never asserts that window.location was set to '/article/5'. Add the missing assertion.\n\nAlso verify that flushMarkReadQueue was triggered (the article id appears in the batch-read POST body).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:59Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:59Z"}

new_value: Added 5 new tests for markAsRead: feed path (POST to /api/feeds/{id}/read-all, reload), global path (POST to /api/articles/read-all, reload), reload fallback when findNextUnreadFolder returns null, error path (console.error + showToast, no reload), and dropdown close (.open class removal). All organized under a shared describe block with helper function.
Fixed openArticle test to assert window.location === '/article/5' and verify flushMarkReadQueue sends batch-read POST with ids:[5]. Also added DOM setup for article card.

{"notes":"## g2q.1 + g2q.2: Error paths and DOM mutations
- JSDOM normalizes self-closing SVG tags (<path .../> → <path ...></path>), so innerHTML assertions must use toContain with distinguishing path data substrings rather than exact SVG constant matching.
- toast.js mock was added (vi.mock('./toast.js')) to assert showToast calls in error paths.
- The api() wrapper in api.js throws on !res.ok, so error path tests mock fetch to return { ok: false, status: 500, text: ... } which triggers the catch blocks.
- updateCounts, updateQueueCacheIfStandalone, and updateReadButton were already mocked; just needed to import them in the test file to assert they were called.
**Correction**: The test environment is happy-dom (not jsdom as stated above). The self-closing SVG tag normalization behavior is the same in both.
## g2q.3 + g2q.4: markAsRead paths and openArticle assertion
- markAsRead tests share a beforeEach that sets up location with a trackable reload mock and a makeDropdown helper to reduce boilerplate.
- For the reload fallback test, window.location must be an object with a reload function (not just a string) since markAsRead calls both location.href= and location.reload().
- openArticle sets window.location as a string assignment (not .href), so the assertion is `expect(window.location).toBe('/article/5')` — requires Object.defineProperty with writable:true."}

- The api() wrapper in api.js throws on !res.ok, so error path tests mock fetch to return { ok: false, status: 500, text: ... } which triggers the catch blocks.
- updateCounts, updateQueueCacheIfStandalone, and updateReadButton were already mocked; just needed to import them in the test file to assert they were called.
**Correction**: The test environment is happy-dom (not jsdom as stated above). The self-closing SVG tag normalization behavior is the same in both.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:30Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:06:55Z"}

1. **Error paths** — add API failure tests for markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue, and markAsRead. Each should verify console.error and showToast are called.

2. **DOM mutation assertions** — strengthen existing happy-path tests to verify class toggles, aria-labels, innerHTML updates, and updateCounts/updateReadButton calls.

3. **Performance** — replace real-timer sleeps in initQueueState tests with microtask flushing.

4. **Missing delegations** — add initArticleActionListeners tests for toggle-read, toggle-star, and toggle-queue data-action handlers.","acceptance_criteria":"- All 4 core actions (markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue) have error-path tests

- markRead test verifies card gets .read class
- markAsRead tests cover feed path, global path, reload fallback, and error path
- openArticle test asserts window.location value
- initArticleActionListeners has tests for toggle-read, toggle-star, toggle-queue delegations
- initQueueState tests complete in <10ms total (no real sleeps)
- All tests pass, make check clean","notes":"## g2q.1 + g2q.2: Error paths and DOM mutations
- JSDOM normalizes self-closing SVG tags (<path .../> → <path ...></path>), so innerHTML assertions must use toContain with distinguishing path data substrings rather than exact SVG constant matching.

{"id":"feedreader-xzaw","title":"[epic] ES Modules Refactor","description":"Refactor `srv/static/app.js` (2277 lines, plain `<script>`) into ES modules.
**Goals:**
1. Coverage — V8 coverage works with real ES module imports
2. Testability — direct imports replace the `new Function()` eval hack
3. Maintainability — split monolith into focused, navigable modules
4. Tooling — static analysis, go-to-definition, dead code detection
**Final state:**
- 23 ES modules in `srv/static/modules/`, each with a companion `.test.js`
- 425 tests across 23 test files
- `app.js` is a 126-line entry point (down from 2277 original)
- Zero inline event handlers — all use `data-action` + `addEventListener`
- Zero `window.X` global exports
- Import maps provide cache busting for all module files
- Coverage: 82% statements, 85% functions (modules only)
See `docs/ES_MODULES_PLAN.md` for full design rationale.
See `docs/BENCHMARK_BASELINE.md` for performance measurements.","design":"# ES Modules Refactor Plan

Refactor `srv/static/app.js` (2277 lines, plain `<script>`) into ES modules.

## Goals

1. **Coverage** — V8 coverage works with real ES module imports
2. **Testability** — direct imports replace the `new Function()` eval hack
3. **Maintainability** — split monolith into focused, navigable modules
4. **Tooling** — static analysis, go-to-definition, dead code detection

## Current State (after Phase 4)

- `app.js` is a 363-line entry point loaded as `<script type="module">`
- 20 ES modules in `srv/static/modules/`, each with a companion `.test.js`
- 390 tests across 20 test files, all using direct ES module imports
- Zero inline event handlers — all use `data-action` + `addEventListener`
- Zero `window.X` global exports
- Import maps provide cache busting for all module files
- 82% statement / 85% function coverage on modules
- 1 `setSidebarLoadCategory()` late-binding call remains (legitimate top-down wiring)
- 2 shared leaf modules (`feed-errors.js`, `read-button.js`) break former cycles
- 1 safe circular ES module import (`articles ↔ pagination`) with hoisted functions

## Proposed Module Structure

```
srv/static/
  app.js              → thin entry point (imports modules, runs init)
  modules/
    api.js            → api() fetch wrapper
    settings.js       → getSetting, saveSetting, applyUserPreferences,
                        applyHideReadArticles, applyHideEmptyFeeds
    utils.js          → formatTimeAgo, formatLocalDate, stripHtml,
                        truncateText, getArticleSortTime
    icons.js          → SVG constant strings
    articles.js       → renderArticleActions, buildArticleCardHtml,
                        renderArticles, updateReadButton,
                        showArticlesLoading, updateAllReadMessage,
                        showReadArticles, showHiddenArticles,
                        processEmbeds, extractYouTubeId
    article-actions.js→ markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue,
                        markReadSilent, openArticle, openArticleExternal,
                        markAsRead, auto-mark-read observer
    pagination.js     → cursor state, loadMoreArticles, checkScrollForMore,
                        updatePaginationCursor, updateEndOfArticlesIndicator
    sidebar.js        → toggleSidebar, setSidebarActive, navigateFolder,
                        toggleFolderCollapse, collapseFolder
    feeds.js          → loadFeedArticles, loadCategoryArticles,
                        refreshFeed, deleteFeed, editFeed, saveFeed,
                        filterFeeds, setFeedCategory, feed error banner,
                        createEditFeedModal, closeEditModal
    folders.js        → openCreateFolderModal, closeCreateFolderModal,
                        submitCreateFolder, renameCategory,
                        unparentCategory, deleteCategory
    counts.js         → updateCounts, updateFeedStatusCell,
                        updateFeedErrors
    views.js          → setView, getViewScope, initView,
                        migrateLegacyViewDefaults, getDefaultViewForScope,
                        applyDefaultViewForScope
    drag-drop.js      → initDragDrop, initFolderDragDrop,
                        syncFolderOrder, reorderElements,
                        getDragAfterElementAmongSiblings

    queue.js          → queuedArticleIds, queuedIdsReady, queueNext
    settings-page.js  → initSettingsPage, runCleanup, newsletter functions

                        cacheQueueForOffline, replayPendingActions
    dropdown.js       → toggleDropdown, click-outside listener
    timestamps.js     → initTimestampTooltips
    scraper-page.js   → scraper tab switching, schema panel, config modal
                        (currently inline in scrapers.html <script>)
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Infrastructure (non-breaking)

1. Create `modules/` directory
2. Extract pure utility modules first (`utils.js`, `icons.js`, `api.js`)
   — these have zero DOM dependencies and are easiest to test
3. Update `vitest.config.mjs` to include `modules/` in coverage
4. Write module-style tests that directly import from the new files
5. Verify old tests still pass (both test styles coexist temporarily)

### Phase 2: Extract stateful modules

6. Extract `settings.js`, `views.js`, `dropdown.js`, `timestamps.js`
7. Extract `articles.js`, `article-actions.js`, `pagination.js`
8. Extract `sidebar.js`, `feeds.js`, `folders.js`, `counts.js`
9. Extract `drag-drop.js`, `opml.js`, `queue.js`
10. Extract `settings-page.js`, `offline.js`

During this phase, `app.js` gradually shrinks as code moves out. Each
extracted module exports its public functions. The entry point imports
and either calls them or registers them.

### Phase 3: Eliminate inline handlers

11. Replace `onclick="fn()"` in templates with `data-action` attributes
    or stable selectors, and wire them up via `addEventListener` in the
    entry point or per-page init functions
12. Replace `onclick` strings built in JS (article cards, modals) with
    DOM creation + `addEventListener`
13. Remove the ESLint `varsIgnorePattern` whitelist
14. Remove all `window.X = X` assignments (nothing needs to be global)

### Phase 4: Finalize

15. Change `<script src="/static/app.js">` to
    `<script type="module" src="/static/app.js">` in `base.html`
16. Delete `test-helper.js` and the eval-based test machinery
17. Rewrite tests as direct ES module imports
18. Verify coverage reports meaningful numbers
19. Update `AGENTS.md` code layout docs

### Phase 5: Eliminate late-bound dependencies

The `setXxxDeps()` pattern was used during Phase 2 to break circular
import dependencies. Of the 6 uses, only 3 are actual cycles — the
other 5 are unnecessary and can be replaced with direct imports. The
3 real cycles can be eliminated through small restructurings.

**Non-circular (replace with direct imports):**

- `article-actions` → `counts.updateCounts` — no cycle
- `article-actions` → `offline.updateQueueCacheIfStandalone` — no cycle
- `counts` → `articles.applyUserPreferences` — no cycle
- `offline` → `counts.updateCounts` — no cycle
- `queue` → `offline.updateQueueCacheIfStandalone` — no cycle

**Real cycles (restructure to eliminate):**

20. `article-actions ↔ articles`: `article-actions` needs
    `updateReadButton` from `articles`, and `articles` imports
    `queuedArticleIds`, `queuedIdsReady`, `initAutoMarkRead` from
    `article-actions`. Fix: extract `updateReadButton` into a small
    shared module (e.g. `read-button.js`) — it's a pure DOM
    manipulation function that updates a button's icon and data
    attributes. Both modules import from it.

21. `articles ↔ pagination`: `pagination` imports
    `buildArticleCardHtml`, `processEmbeds`, `applyUserPreferences`,
    `getShowingHiddenArticles` from `articles`, while `articles`
    needs `updatePaginationCursor`, `updateEndOfArticlesIndicator`,
    `setPaginationState` from `pagination`. Fix: the pagination state
    functions that `articles` needs are really part of "rendering
    articles" — move them into `articles.js`, making `pagination.js`
    a pure "load more" module that calls into `articles`.
    Alternatively, extract the shared rendering functions
    (`buildArticleCardHtml`, `processEmbeds`) into a lower-level
    `article-rendering.js`.

22. `counts ↔ feeds` and `sidebar ↔ feeds`: `feeds` imports
    `updateCounts` from `counts` and `setSidebarActive` from
    `sidebar`. `counts` needs `showFeedErrorBanner` /
    `removeFeedErrorBanner` from `feeds`. `sidebar` needs
    `loadCategoryArticles` from `feeds`. Fix: move
    `showFeedErrorBanner`/`removeFeedErrorBanner` into `counts.js`
    (or a new `feed-errors.js`) since they're only called from
    `updateFeedErrors` in counts. For sidebar → feeds,
    `loadCategoryArticles` is a high-level orchestration function —
    keep the callback from `app.js` (this is legitimate top-down
    wiring, not a circular dependency hack).

After this phase, the only remaining `setXxxDeps` call should be
`setSidebarLoadCategory` in `app.js`, which is genuine top-down
wiring from the entry point.

### Phase 6: Clean up app.js entry point

`app.js` still contains ~160 lines of page-specific logic that
belongs in modules. The entry point should only import, wire
dependencies, and call init functions.

**Move to existing modules:**

23. Add feed form handler (72 lines) → `feeds.js` as
    `initAddFeedForm()`. Contains Reddit URL building, HuggingFace
    config construction, category assignment — all feed-specific
    logic with no entry-point concerns.

24. Feed item click handler (12 lines) → `feeds.js` as part of
    `initFeedActionListeners()`. SPA feed navigation that already
    calls `loadFeedArticles`.

25. Sidebar mobile close (8 lines) → `sidebar.js` as part of
    `initSidebarListeners()`. Sidebar behavior.

26. Pagination bootstrap + scroll listener (11 lines) →
    `pagination.js` as `initPagination()`. Reads DOM for initial
    cursor state and registers the scroll listener — pure pagination
    concerns.

27. Queue hydration (16 lines) → `article-actions.js` as
    `initQueueState()`. Fetches queue IDs from the API, populates
    `queuedArticleIds`, and renders action-button placeholders.
    Already owns that state.

28. Drag prevention (4 lines) → `drag-drop.js` as part of its init.

**Move to new module:**

29. Search handler (36 lines) → new `search.js` module. Self-contained
    feature with its own state (`originalHTML`, `searchAbort`,
    debounce timer) and no dependency on other app.js code.

**Keep in app.js:**

- Imports (~25 lines after cleanup)
- Listener init calls (~16 lines)
- Expand active folder parents (8 lines) — cross-cutting DOM init
- One-liner init calls: `initView`, `initFolderDragDrop`, page inits
- `setInterval(updateCounts, 60000)` — app-level polling policy
- `setSidebarLoadCategory` wiring (legitimate top-down callback)

After this phase, `app.js` should be ~120 lines — almost entirely
imports, init calls, and DOMContentLoaded sequencing.

Phase 6 also adds `<link rel="modulepreload">` hints in `base.html`
for all 20 modules. The ES module import waterfall added +328ms to
cold DOMContentLoaded (the browser discovers imports sequentially
across 3 depth levels). Modulepreload lets the browser fetch all
modules in parallel upfront, eliminating the waterfall.

## Key Decisions

### No bundler

Modern browsers handle ES module imports natively. This is a single-user
app behind a proxy — the extra HTTP requests for ~15 module files are
irrelevant. A bundler adds build complexity for no real benefit here.

### Transitional `window` exports

During Phases 2–3, functions still called from `onclick` attributes must
remain on `window`. The entry point can do:
```js
import { toggleStar } from './modules/article-actions.js';
window.toggleStar = toggleStar;
```
This is removed in Phase 3 when inline handlers are eliminated.

### Shared state

Mutable state (`paginationCursorTime`, `queuedArticleIds`, etc.) lives
in the module that owns it, exposed via getter/setter functions rather
than direct variable access. This makes dependencies explicit.

### Test migration

Tests can be migrated incrementally. As each module is extracted, its
tests switch from `window.fn()` to `import { fn }`. The eval-based
test helper remains functional for not-yet-extracted code.

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Circular dependencies between modules | 3 real cycles identified (see Phase 5); resolved via small restructurings — extract shared leaf modules or consolidate tightly-coupled state |
| Breaking inline handlers mid-migration | Transitional `window` exports keep everything working |
| `<script type="module">` is deferred (runs later than classic scripts) | This app already uses `DOMContentLoaded`; deferred execution is equivalent |
| `type="module"` doesn't run in very old browsers | Not a concern — single-user app on modern browser |
| Large diff is hard to review | Phase-by-phase commits, each independently passing `make check` |","acceptance_criteria":"- app.js is a thin entry point (~120 lines): imports, init calls, DOMContentLoaded sequencing
- All JS logic lives in ES modules under srv/static/modules/
- Every module has a companion .test.js with meaningful unit tests
- Zero inline event handlers (onclick/onchange/etc) in templates or JS-built HTML
- Zero window.X global exports
- Import maps with content hashes for cache busting
- No setXxxDeps late-binding hacks (except setSidebarLoadCategory for legitimate top-down wiring)
- make check passes clean
- Coverage: >80% statements, >85% functions on modules","notes":"# ES Modules Migration — Working Memory

# Page Load Benchmark — Baseline

Captured **2026-02-24** before ES modules refactor.
Single `<script>` tag loading `app.js` (87 KB raw, 26 KB gzipped).

## Navigation Timing

| Metric              | Time   |
|---------------------|--------|
| TTFB                | 124ms  |
| DOM Interactive      | 218ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 226ms  |
| Load Complete        | 231ms  |
| First Paint          | 236ms  |
| First Contentful Paint | 236ms |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset     | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) | Duration |
|-----------|----------|-----------------|----------|
| app.js    | 87 KB    | 26 KB           | 18ms     |
| style.css | 53 KB    | 13 KB           | 11ms     |
| script.js | 547 B    | —               | —        |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric          | Value      |
|-----------------|------------|
| Total requests  | 35         |
| Total transfer  | 185 KB     |
| JS requests     | 1 (app.js) |
| Favicon requests | 33        |

## Notes

- Measured via browser Performance API through mitmdump reverse proxy
  (localhost:3000 → localhost:8000)
- 33 of 35 requests are sidebar favicon images (~1 KB each, 82–224ms each
  due to DB lookups / proxying)
- One fetch request to `/api/queue` (120 KB, 92ms) fires after DOMContentLoaded
- JS/CSS load times are negligible (<20ms) since it's all localhost
- After the refactor, expect ~15 JS module requests replacing the single
  app.js request. Each module will be 1–10 KB. Impact on load time should
  be minimal given localhost latency.

## How to Re-measure

```bash
# Start auth proxy if not running
mitmdump --mode reverse:http://localhost:8000 --listen-port 3000 \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Email/test@example.com' \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Userid/testuser123'
```

Then in the browser console on `http://localhost:3000/`:

```js
const nav = performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0];
const paint = performance.getEntriesByType('paint');
const resources = performance.getEntriesByType('resource');
console.table({
  ttfb: Math.round(nav.responseStart),
  domInteractive: Math.round(nav.domInteractive),
  domContentLoaded: Math.round(nav.domContentLoadedEventEnd),
  loadComplete: Math.round(nav.loadEventEnd),
});
console.log('Paint:', paint.map(p => `${p.name}: ${Math.round(p.startTime)}ms`));
console.log(`${resources.length} requests, ${Math.round(resources.reduce((s,r) => s + (r.transferSize||0), 0) / 1024)} KB`);
```

# Page Load Benchmark — After ES Modules Refactor

Captured **2026-02-25** after completing Phases 1–4 of the ES modules
refactor. `app.js` is now a 363-line entry point loading 20 ES modules
via `<script type="module">` with import maps for cache busting.

## Navigation Timing (cold load)

| Metric               | Time   | Δ from baseline |
|----------------------|--------|----------------|
| TTFB                 | 257ms  | +133ms         |
| DOM Interactive      | 378ms  | +160ms         |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 554ms  | +328ms         |
| Load Complete        | 570ms  | +339ms         |
| First Paint          | 404ms  | +168ms         |
| First Contentful Paint | 404ms | +168ms         |

## Navigation Timing (warm/cached load)

| Metric               | Time       |
|----------------------|------------|
| TTFB                 | 223–264ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 287–331ms  |
| Load Complete        | 293–336ms  |
| First Paint          | 292–336ms  |
| Transfer             | **0 KB**   |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset                  | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) |
|------------------------|----------|----------------|
| app.js                 | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/feeds.js       | 17 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/articles.js    | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/article-actions.js | 12 KB | 3 KB          |
| modules/scraper-page.js | 11 KB   | 3 KB           |
| modules/drag-drop.js   | 9 KB     | 2 KB           |
| modules/offline.js     | 8 KB     | 3 KB           |
| 14 smaller modules     | 36 KB    | 12 KB          |
| **Total JS**           | **119 KB** | **35 KB**    |
| style.css              | 52 KB    | 13 KB          |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric           | Value          | Δ from baseline |
|------------------|----------------|----------------|
| Total requests   | 58             | +23            |
| JS requests      | 21             | +20            |
| JS raw size      | 119 KB         | +32 KB (+37%)  |
| JS gzip size     | 35 KB          | +9 KB (+35%)   |
| Favicon requests | 35             | +2             |

## Analysis

**Cold load is slower** due to the ES module import waterfall. The
browser discovers imports sequentially as it parses each module,
creating a dependency chain. With 20 modules and a 3-level import
depth, this adds multiple sequential round trips even on localhost.

**Warm load is fast** — import maps with content hashes enable
immutable caching (`max-age=31536000`), so subsequent visits transfer
0 bytes and load in ~300ms, comparable to the pre-refactor cold load.

**Raw JS size grew 37%** — import/export statements and late-binding
boilerplate (`setXxxDeps` patterns) add overhead. Gzip is also less
effective across many small files vs. one large file.

## Potential Optimizations

- **`<link rel="modulepreload">`** for each module in `base.html`
  would eliminate the waterfall by fetching all modules in parallel
- **Phase 5/6 cleanup** will reduce module count and remove
  late-binding boilerplate
- **Bundling** for production remains an option but was explicitly
  deferred (see ES_MODULES_PLAN.md)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:32:00Z","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:45:17Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T23:32:38Z","close_reason":"All 6 phases completed"}

{"id":"feedreader-maz","title":"Fix import map vs modulepreload ordering in base.html","description":"**Problem:**
Browser console shows ~15 warnings on every page load:
`An import map rule for specifier 'https://lynx-fairy.exe.xyz/static/modules/X.js' was removed, as it conflicted with already resolved module specifiers.`

**Root Cause:**
In `base.html`, the `{{modulePreloadTags}}` output (which generates `<link rel="modulepreload">` elements) appears BEFORE the `<script type="importmap">` tag. Per the HTML spec, import maps must be processed before any module loading begins. When the browser encounters modulepreload links first, it starts fetching and resolving those modules. By the time it reaches the import map, those module specifiers are already resolved, so the import map rules for those specifiers are discarded.

The import map exists to remap bare module paths (e.g., `/static/modules/api.js`) to versioned URLs (`/static/modules/api.js?v=hash`) for cache busting. Modules import each other using relative paths (`./api.js`) which resolve to the unversioned URLs. Without the import map being active, these inter-module imports bypass cache busting.

**Introduced in:**
- Phase 4 (commit 98218de): Added import map
- Phase 6 (commit cd8c931): Added modulepreload hints — placed BEFORE the import map

**Fix:**
Move the import map `<script>` tag before the modulepreload `<link>` tags in `base.html`. The correct order is:
1. `<script type="importmap">` — must come first
2. `<link rel="modulepreload">` — can hint at versioned URLs
3. `<script type="module">` — entry point

The modulepreload hrefs already use versioned URLs (`?v=hash`), so they will still work correctly as cache-busted preload hints. The import map will be active when the entry point script triggers module resolution via relative paths.

**Verification:**
- No console warnings about conflicting import map rules
- Network tab shows versioned URLs for all module requests
- Cache busting still works (change a module, reload, new version fetched)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:01:03Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:01:03Z"}

# Page Load Benchmark — Baseline

Captured **2026-02-24** before ES modules refactor.
Single `<script>` tag loading `app.js` (87 KB raw, 26 KB gzipped).

## Navigation Timing

| Metric              | Time   |
|---------------------|--------|
| TTFB                | 124ms  |
| DOM Interactive      | 218ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 226ms  |
| Load Complete        | 231ms  |
| First Paint          | 236ms  |
| First Contentful Paint | 236ms |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset     | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) | Duration |
|-----------|----------|-----------------|----------|
| app.js    | 87 KB    | 26 KB           | 18ms     |
| style.css | 53 KB    | 13 KB           | 11ms     |
| script.js | 547 B    | —               | —        |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric          | Value      |
|-----------------|------------|
| Total requests  | 35         |
| Total transfer  | 185 KB     |
| JS requests     | 1 (app.js) |
| Favicon requests | 33        |

## Notes

- Measured via browser Performance API through mitmdump reverse proxy
  (localhost:3000 → localhost:8000)
- 33 of 35 requests are sidebar favicon images (~1 KB each, 82–224ms each
  due to DB lookups / proxying)
- One fetch request to `/api/queue` (120 KB, 92ms) fires after DOMContentLoaded
- JS/CSS load times are negligible (<20ms) since it's all localhost
- After the refactor, expect ~15 JS module requests replacing the single
  app.js request. Each module will be 1–10 KB. Impact on load time should
  be minimal given localhost latency.

## How to Re-measure

```bash
# Start auth proxy if not running
mitmdump --mode reverse:http://localhost:8000 --listen-port 3000 \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Email/test@example.com' \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Userid/testuser123'
```

Then in the browser console on `http://localhost:3000/`:

```js
const nav = performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0];
const paint = performance.getEntriesByType('paint');
const resources = performance.getEntriesByType('resource');
console.table({
  ttfb: Math.round(nav.responseStart),
  domInteractive: Math.round(nav.domInteractive),
  domContentLoaded: Math.round(nav.domContentLoadedEventEnd),
  loadComplete: Math.round(nav.loadEventEnd),
});
console.log('Paint:', paint.map(p => `${p.name}: ${Math.round(p.startTime)}ms`));
console.log(`${resources.length} requests, ${Math.round(resources.reduce((s,r) => s + (r.transferSize||0), 0) / 1024)} KB`);
```

# Page Load Benchmark — After ES Modules Refactor

Captured **2026-02-25** after completing Phases 1–4 of the ES modules
refactor. `app.js` is now a 363-line entry point loading 20 ES modules
via `<script type="module">` with import maps for cache busting.

## Navigation Timing (cold load)

| Metric               | Time   | Δ from baseline |
|----------------------|--------|----------------|
| TTFB                 | 257ms  | +133ms         |
| DOM Interactive      | 378ms  | +160ms         |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 554ms  | +328ms         |
| Load Complete        | 570ms  | +339ms         |
| First Paint          | 404ms  | +168ms         |
| First Contentful Paint | 404ms | +168ms         |

## Navigation Timing (warm/cached load)

| Metric               | Time       |
|----------------------|------------|
| TTFB                 | 223–264ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 287–331ms  |
| Load Complete        | 293–336ms  |
| First Paint          | 292–336ms  |
| Transfer             | **0 KB**   |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset                  | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) |
|------------------------|----------|----------------|
| app.js                 | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/feeds.js       | 17 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/articles.js    | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/article-actions.js | 12 KB | 3 KB          |
| modules/scraper-page.js | 11 KB   | 3 KB           |
| modules/drag-drop.js   | 9 KB     | 2 KB           |
| modules/offline.js     | 8 KB     | 3 KB           |
| 14 smaller modules     | 36 KB    | 12 KB          |
| **Total JS**           | **119 KB** | **35 KB**    |
| style.css              | 52 KB    | 13 KB          |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric           | Value          | Δ from baseline |
|------------------|----------------|----------------|
| Total requests   | 58             | +23            |
| JS requests      | 21             | +20            |
| JS raw size      | 119 KB         | +32 KB (+37%)  |
| JS gzip size     | 35 KB          | +9 KB (+35%)   |
| Favicon requests | 35             | +2             |

## Analysis

**Cold load is slower** due to the ES module import waterfall. The
browser discovers imports sequentially as it parses each module,
creating a dependency chain. With 20 modules and a 3-level import
depth, this adds multiple sequential round trips even on localhost.

**Warm load is fast** — import maps with content hashes enable
immutable caching (`max-age=31536000`), so subsequent visits transfer
0 bytes and load in ~300ms, comparable to the pre-refactor cold load.

**Raw JS size grew 37%** — import/export statements and late-binding
boilerplate (`setXxxDeps` patterns) add overhead. Gzip is also less
effective across many small files vs. one large file.

## Potential Optimizations

- **`<link rel="modulepreload">`** for each module in `base.html`
  would eliminate the waterfall by fetching all modules in parallel
- **Phase 5/6 cleanup** will reduce module count and remove
  late-binding boilerplate
- **Bundling** for production remains an option but was explicitly
  deferred (see ES_MODULES_PLAN.md)
## feedreader-xzaw.1: SPA sidebar navigation
- Created spa-nav.js module that intercepts .nav-item clicks and popstate events
- Used dynamic imports in popstate handler to avoid circular deps with feeds.js
- Added pushState option param to loadFeedArticles/loadCategoryArticles to prevent double history entries during popstate
- Changed sidebar.js navigateFolder check from #articles-list to .articles-view for broader compatibility
- The dropdown and header actions persist across SPA navigations since only #articles-list content is swapped
- Pages with different layouts (queue, history, settings, feeds, scrapers) still use full navigation"}

Refactor `srv/static/app.js` (2277 lines, plain `<script>`) into ES modules.

## Goals

1. **Coverage** — V8 coverage works with real ES module imports
2. **Testability** — direct imports replace the `new Function()` eval hack
3. **Maintainability** — split monolith into focused, navigable modules
4. **Tooling** — static analysis, go-to-definition, dead code detection

## Current State (after Phase 4)

- `app.js` is a 363-line entry point loaded as `<script type="module">`
- 20 ES modules in `srv/static/modules/`, each with a companion `.test.js`
- 390 tests across 20 test files, all using direct ES module imports
- Zero inline event handlers — all use `data-action` + `addEventListener`
- Zero `window.X` global exports
- Import maps provide cache busting for all module files
- 82% statement / 85% function coverage on modules
- 1 `setSidebarLoadCategory()` late-binding call remains (legitimate top-down wiring)
- 2 shared leaf modules (`feed-errors.js`, `read-button.js`) break former cycles
- 1 safe circular ES module import (`articles ↔ pagination`) with hoisted functions

## Proposed Module Structure

```
srv/static/
  app.js              → thin entry point (imports modules, runs init)
  modules/
    api.js            → api() fetch wrapper
    settings.js       → getSetting, saveSetting, applyUserPreferences,
                        applyHideReadArticles, applyHideEmptyFeeds
    utils.js          → formatTimeAgo, formatLocalDate, stripHtml,
                        truncateText, getArticleSortTime
    icons.js          → SVG constant strings
    articles.js       → renderArticleActions, buildArticleCardHtml,
                        renderArticles, updateReadButton,
                        showArticlesLoading, updateAllReadMessage,
                        showReadArticles, showHiddenArticles,
                        processEmbeds, extractYouTubeId
    article-actions.js→ markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue,
                        markReadSilent, openArticle, openArticleExternal,
                        markAsRead, auto-mark-read observer
    pagination.js     → cursor state, loadMoreArticles, checkScrollForMore,
                        updatePaginationCursor, updateEndOfArticlesIndicator
    sidebar.js        → toggleSidebar, setSidebarActive, navigateFolder,
                        toggleFolderCollapse, collapseFolder
    feeds.js          → loadFeedArticles, loadCategoryArticles,
                        refreshFeed, deleteFeed, editFeed, saveFeed,
                        filterFeeds, setFeedCategory, feed error banner,
                        createEditFeedModal, closeEditModal
    folders.js        → openCreateFolderModal, closeCreateFolderModal,
                        submitCreateFolder, renameCategory,
                        unparentCategory, deleteCategory
    counts.js         → updateCounts, updateFeedStatusCell,
                        updateFeedErrors
    views.js          → setView, getViewScope, initView,
                        migrateLegacyViewDefaults, getDefaultViewForScope,
                        applyDefaultViewForScope
    drag-drop.js      → initDragDrop, initFolderDragDrop,
                        syncFolderOrder, reorderElements,
                        getDragAfterElementAmongSiblings
    opml.js           → exportOPML, importOPML
    queue.js          → queuedArticleIds, queuedIdsReady, queueNext
    settings-page.js  → initSettingsPage, runCleanup, newsletter functions
    offline.js        → service worker, online/offline handling,
                        cacheQueueForOffline, replayPendingActions
    dropdown.js       → toggleDropdown, click-outside listener
    timestamps.js     → initTimestampTooltips
    scraper-page.js   → scraper tab switching, schema panel, config modal
                        (currently inline in scrapers.html <script>)
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Infrastructure (non-breaking)

1. Create `modules/` directory
2. Extract pure utility modules first (`utils.js`, `icons.js`, `api.js`)
   — these have zero DOM dependencies and are easiest to test
3. Update `vitest.config.mjs` to include `modules/` in coverage
4. Write module-style tests that directly import from the new files
5. Verify old tests still pass (both test styles coexist temporarily)

### Phase 2: Extract stateful modules

6. Extract `settings.js`, `views.js`, `dropdown.js`, `timestamps.js`
7. Extract `articles.js`, `article-actions.js`, `pagination.js`
8. Extract `sidebar.js`, `feeds.js`, `folders.js`, `counts.js`
9. Extract `drag-drop.js`, `opml.js`, `queue.js`
10. Extract `settings-page.js`, `offline.js`

During this phase, `app.js` gradually shrinks as code moves out. Each
extracted module exports its public functions. The entry point imports
and either calls them or registers them.

### Phase 3: Eliminate inline handlers

11. Replace `onclick="fn()"` in templates with `data-action` attributes
    or stable selectors, and wire them up via `addEventListener` in the
    entry point or per-page init functions
12. Replace `onclick` strings built in JS (article cards, modals) with
    DOM creation + `addEventListener`
13. Remove the ESLint `varsIgnorePattern` whitelist
14. Remove all `window.X = X` assignments (nothing needs to be global)

### Phase 4: Finalize

15. Change `<script src="/static/app.js">` to
    `<script type="module" src="/static/app.js">` in `base.html`
16. Delete `test-helper.js` and the eval-based test machinery
17. Rewrite tests as direct ES module imports
18. Verify coverage reports meaningful numbers
19. Update `AGENTS.md` code layout docs

### Phase 5: Eliminate late-bound dependencies

The `setXxxDeps()` pattern was used during Phase 2 to break circular
import dependencies. Of the 6 uses, only 3 are actual cycles — the
other 5 are unnecessary and can be replaced with direct imports. The
3 real cycles can be eliminated through small restructurings.

**Non-circular (replace with direct imports):**

- `article-actions` → `counts.updateCounts` — no cycle
- `article-actions` → `offline.updateQueueCacheIfStandalone` — no cycle
- `counts` → `articles.applyUserPreferences` — no cycle
- `offline` → `counts.updateCounts` — no cycle
- `queue` → `offline.updateQueueCacheIfStandalone` — no cycle

**Real cycles (restructure to eliminate):**

20. `article-actions ↔ articles`: `article-actions` needs
    `updateReadButton` from `articles`, and `articles` imports
    `queuedArticleIds`, `queuedIdsReady`, `initAutoMarkRead` from
    `article-actions`. Fix: extract `updateReadButton` into a small
    shared module (e.g. `read-button.js`) — it's a pure DOM
    manipulation function that updates a button's icon and data
    attributes. Both modules import from it.

21. `articles ↔ pagination`: `pagination` imports
    `buildArticleCardHtml`, `processEmbeds`, `applyUserPreferences`,
    `getShowingHiddenArticles` from `articles`, while `articles`
    needs `updatePaginationCursor`, `updateEndOfArticlesIndicator`,
    `setPaginationState` from `pagination`. Fix: the pagination state
    functions that `articles` needs are really part of "rendering
    articles" — move them into `articles.js`, making `pagination.js`
    a pure "load more" module that calls into `articles`.
    Alternatively, extract the shared rendering functions
    (`buildArticleCardHtml`, `processEmbeds`) into a lower-level
    `article-rendering.js`.

22. `counts ↔ feeds` and `sidebar ↔ feeds`: `feeds` imports
    `updateCounts` from `counts` and `setSidebarActive` from
    `sidebar`. `counts` needs `showFeedErrorBanner` /
    `removeFeedErrorBanner` from `feeds`. `sidebar` needs
    `loadCategoryArticles` from `feeds`. Fix: move
    `showFeedErrorBanner`/`removeFeedErrorBanner` into `counts.js`
    (or a new `feed-errors.js`) since they're only called from
    `updateFeedErrors` in counts. For sidebar → feeds,
    `loadCategoryArticles` is a high-level orchestration function —
    keep the callback from `app.js` (this is legitimate top-down
    wiring, not a circular dependency hack).

After this phase, the only remaining `setXxxDeps` call should be
`setSidebarLoadCategory` in `app.js`, which is genuine top-down
wiring from the entry point.

### Phase 6: Clean up app.js entry point

`app.js` still contains ~160 lines of page-specific logic that
belongs in modules. The entry point should only import, wire
dependencies, and call init functions.

**Move to existing modules:**

23. Add feed form handler (72 lines) → `feeds.js` as
    `initAddFeedForm()`. Contains Reddit URL building, HuggingFace
    config construction, category assignment — all feed-specific
    logic with no entry-point concerns.

24. Feed item click handler (12 lines) → `feeds.js` as part of
    `initFeedActionListeners()`. SPA feed navigation that already
    calls `loadFeedArticles`.

25. Sidebar mobile close (8 lines) → `sidebar.js` as part of
    `initSidebarListeners()`. Sidebar behavior.

26. Pagination bootstrap + scroll listener (11 lines) →
    `pagination.js` as `initPagination()`. Reads DOM for initial
    cursor state and registers the scroll listener — pure pagination
    concerns.

27. Queue hydration (16 lines) → `article-actions.js` as
    `initQueueState()`. Fetches queue IDs from the API, populates
    `queuedArticleIds`, and renders action-button placeholders.
    Already owns that state.

28. Drag prevention (4 lines) → `drag-drop.js` as part of its init.

**Move to new module:**

29. Search handler (36 lines) → new `search.js` module. Self-contained
    feature with its own state (`originalHTML`, `searchAbort`,
    debounce timer) and no dependency on other app.js code.

**Keep in app.js:**

- Imports (~25 lines after cleanup)
- Listener init calls (~16 lines)
- Expand active folder parents (8 lines) — cross-cutting DOM init
- One-liner init calls: `initView`, `initFolderDragDrop`, page inits
- `setInterval(updateCounts, 60000)` — app-level polling policy
- `setSidebarLoadCategory` wiring (legitimate top-down callback)

After this phase, `app.js` should be ~120 lines — almost entirely
imports, init calls, and DOMContentLoaded sequencing.

Phase 6 also adds `<link rel="modulepreload">` hints in `base.html`
for all 20 modules. The ES module import waterfall added +328ms to
cold DOMContentLoaded (the browser discovers imports sequentially
across 3 depth levels). Modulepreload lets the browser fetch all
modules in parallel upfront, eliminating the waterfall.

## Key Decisions

### No bundler

Modern browsers handle ES module imports natively. This is a single-user
app behind a proxy — the extra HTTP requests for ~15 module files are
irrelevant. A bundler adds build complexity for no real benefit here.

### Transitional `window` exports

During Phases 2–3, functions still called from `onclick` attributes must
remain on `window`. The entry point can do:
```js
import { toggleStar } from './modules/article-actions.js';
window.toggleStar = toggleStar;
```
This is removed in Phase 3 when inline handlers are eliminated.

### Shared state

Mutable state (`paginationCursorTime`, `queuedArticleIds`, etc.) lives
in the module that owns it, exposed via getter/setter functions rather
than direct variable access. This makes dependencies explicit.

### Test migration

Tests can be migrated incrementally. As each module is extracted, its
tests switch from `window.fn()` to `import { fn }`. The eval-based
test helper remains functional for not-yet-extracted code.

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Circular dependencies between modules | 3 real cycles identified (see Phase 5); resolved via small restructurings — extract shared leaf modules or consolidate tightly-coupled state |
| Breaking inline handlers mid-migration | Transitional `window` exports keep everything working |
| `<script type="module">` is deferred (runs later than classic scripts) | This app already uses `DOMContentLoaded`; deferred execution is equivalent |
| `type="module"` doesn't run in very old browsers | Not a concern — single-user app on modern browser |
| Large diff is hard to review | Phase-by-phase commits, each independently passing `make check` |","acceptance_criteria":"- app.js is a thin entry point (~120 lines): imports, init calls, DOMContentLoaded sequencing

- Zero window.X global exports
- Import maps with content hashes for cache busting
- No setXxxDeps late-binding hacks (except setSidebarLoadCategory for legitimate top-down wiring)

**Known issue:** Browser caches ES module files aggressively. The server now sends `max-age=60, must-revalidate` for module files, but browsers that cached modules before the fix may not see updates immediately. A proper solution would be import maps with version hashes. This can be addressed in Phase 4 or as a follow-up.

## Run 13 — Phase 4 completed (import maps, coverage, AGENTS.md)

**Completed:**
- **Import maps with version hashes:** Added `moduleImportMap` template function in `server.go` that generates a JSON import map mapping each module's absolute URL (`/static/modules/foo.js`) to its versioned URL (`/static/modules/foo.js?v=hash`). # Page Load Benchmark — Baseline

Captured **2026-02-24** before ES modules refactor.
Single `<script>` tag loading `app.js` (87 KB raw, 26 KB gzipped).

## Navigation Timing

| Metric              | Time   |
|---------------------|--------|
| TTFB                | 124ms  |
| DOM Interactive      | 218ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 226ms  |
| Load Complete        | 231ms  |
| First Paint          | 236ms  |
| First Contentful Paint | 236ms |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset     | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) | Duration |
|-----------|----------|-----------------|----------|
| app.js    | 87 KB    | 26 KB           | 18ms     |
| style.css | 53 KB    | 13 KB           | 11ms     |
| script.js | 547 B    | —               | —        |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric          | Value      |
|-----------------|------------|
| Total requests  | 35         |
| Total transfer  | 185 KB     |
| JS requests     | 1 (app.js) |
| Favicon requests | 33        |

## Notes

- Measured via browser Performance API through mitmdump reverse proxy
  (localhost:3000 → localhost:8000)
- 33 of 35 requests are sidebar favicon images (~1 KB each, 82–224ms each
  due to DB lookups / proxying)
- One fetch request to `/api/queue` (120 KB, 92ms) fires after DOMContentLoaded
- JS/CSS load times are negligible (<20ms) since it's all localhost
- After the refactor, expect ~15 JS module requests replacing the single
  app.js request. Each module will be 1–10 KB. Impact on load time should
  be minimal given localhost latency.

## How to Re-measure

```bash
# Start auth proxy if not running
mitmdump --mode reverse:http://localhost:8000 --listen-port 3000 \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Email/test@example.com' \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Userid/testuser123'
```

Then in the browser console on `http://localhost:3000/`:

```js
const nav = performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0];
const paint = performance.getEntriesByType('paint');
const resources = performance.getEntriesByType('resource');
console.table({
  ttfb: Math.round(nav.responseStart),
  domInteractive: Math.round(nav.domInteractive),
  domContentLoaded: Math.round(nav.domContentLoadedEventEnd),
  loadComplete: Math.round(nav.loadEventEnd),
});
console.log('Paint:', paint.map(p => `${p.name}: ${Math.round(p.startTime)}ms`));
console.log(`${resources.length} requests, ${Math.round(resources.reduce((s,r) => s + (r.transferSize||0), 0) / 1024)} KB`);
```

# Page Load Benchmark — After ES Modules Refactor

Captured **2026-02-25** after completing Phases 1–4 of the ES modules
refactor. `app.js` is now a 363-line entry point loading 20 ES modules
via `<script type="module">` with import maps for cache busting.

## Navigation Timing (cold load)

| Metric               | Time   | Δ from baseline |
|----------------------|--------|----------------|
| TTFB                 | 257ms  | +133ms         |
| DOM Interactive      | 378ms  | +160ms         |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 554ms  | +328ms         |
| Load Complete        | 570ms  | +339ms         |
| First Paint          | 404ms  | +168ms         |
| First Contentful Paint | 404ms | +168ms         |

## Navigation Timing (warm/cached load)

| Metric               | Time       |
|----------------------|------------|
| TTFB                 | 223–264ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 287–331ms  |
| Load Complete        | 293–336ms  |
| First Paint          | 292–336ms  |
| Transfer             | **0 KB**   |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset                  | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) |
|------------------------|----------|----------------|
| app.js                 | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/feeds.js       | 17 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/articles.js    | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/article-actions.js | 12 KB | 3 KB          |
| modules/scraper-page.js | 11 KB   | 3 KB           |
| modules/drag-drop.js   | 9 KB     | 2 KB           |
| modules/offline.js     | 8 KB     | 3 KB           |
| 14 smaller modules     | 36 KB    | 12 KB          |
| **Total JS**           | **119 KB** | **35 KB**    |
| style.css              | 52 KB    | 13 KB          |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric           | Value          | Δ from baseline |
|------------------|----------------|----------------|
| Total requests   | 58             | +23            |
| JS requests      | 21             | +20            |
| JS raw size      | 119 KB         | +32 KB (+37%)  |
| JS gzip size     | 35 KB          | +9 KB (+35%)   |
| Favicon requests | 35             | +2             |

## Analysis

**Cold load is slower** due to the ES module import waterfall. The
browser discovers imports sequentially as it parses each module,
creating a dependency chain. With 20 modules and a 3-level import
depth, this adds multiple sequential round trips even on localhost.

**Warm load is fast** — import maps with content hashes enable
immutable caching (`max-age=31536000`), so subsequent visits transfer
0 bytes and load in ~300ms, comparable to the pre-refactor cold load.

**Raw JS size grew 37%** — import/export statements and late-binding
boilerplate (`setXxxDeps` patterns) add overhead. Gzip is also less
effective across many small files vs. one large file.

## Potential Optimizations

- **`<link rel="modulepreload">`** for each module in `base.html`
  would eliminate the waterfall by fetching all modules in parallel
- **Phase 5/6 cleanup** will reduce module count and remove
  late-binding boilerplate
- **Bundling** for production remains an option but was explicitly
  deferred (see ES_MODULES_PLAN.md)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:32:00Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:50:58Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T23:32:38Z","close_reason":"All 6 phases completed"}

{"notes":"## g2q.1 + g2q.2: Error paths and DOM mutations
- JSDOM normalizes self-closing SVG tags (<path .../> → <path ...></path>), so innerHTML assertions must use toContain with distinguishing path data substrings rather than exact SVG constant matching.

- markAsRead tests share a beforeEach that sets up location with a trackable reload mock and a makeDropdown helper to reduce boilerplate.
- For the reload fallback test, window.location must be an object with a reload function (not just a string) since markAsRead calls both location.href= and location.reload().
- openArticle sets window.location as a string assignment (not .href), so the assertion is `expect(window.location).toBe('/article/5')` — requires Object.defineProperty with writable:true.
## g2q.5 + g2q.6: Delegation tests and microtask flushing
- Delegation tests use `vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0)` after `.click()` to flush async promise chains (markRead/toggleStar/toggleQueue are async).
- toggleQueue delegation test needs its own fetch mock returning `{ queued: true }` since the response shape differs from the default mock.
- The no-article-id guard test verifies that `Number(undefined)` produces NaN which is falsy, so the early return fires correctly.
- `vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0)` is sufficient to flush the microtask queue for initQueueState's `.then()` chain — no need for real timers or sleep."}

{"notes":"## r4e.1 & r4e.2: editFeed/createEditFeedModal and refreshFeed tests
- createEditFeedModal appends to document.body, so no DOM setup needed in beforeEach — but the modal persists across tests since beforeEach clears body.innerHTML
- editFeed internally calls createEditFeedModal, so testing editFeed also implicitly tests modal creation
- refreshFeed fake timer tests need careful sequencing: await promise first, then advance timers for the restore setTimeout. The pattern is: call function -> advance 1s for polling -> await promise -> assert intermediate state -> advance 2s for restore -> assert final state
- The catch path test is simpler because the error is synchronous (fetch rejects immediately), no polling loop to advance through
## r4e.5 & r4e.6: Side-effect assertions and setTimeout elimination
- Mocking views.js and feed-errors.js at file level works cleanly since feeds.js imports them directly. Pattern: vi.mock('./views.js', () => ({ applyDefaultViewForScope: vi.fn() }))
- vi.waitFor with { interval: 1 } is the right replacement for setTimeout sleeps when waiting for async event handlers. Default 50ms interval defeats the purpose.
- The initAddFeedForm submit handler is async but attached via addEventListener, so we can't await it directly. vi.waitFor polling on the terminal side-effect (reloadMock or showToast) is the clean pattern.
- showFeedErrorBanner test was previously checking DOM output; now checks the mock call since feed-errors.js is mocked. This is more precise and doesn't depend on feed-errors.js implementation details."}

Test cases needed:

new_value: {"assignee":"exe.dev user","status":"in_progress"}

Added 3 tests: saveFeed updates sidebar .feed-name when matching element exists, updates .view-header h1 and document.title when location matches /feed/{id}, does not update header when on a different page.
Added 2 tests: close-edit-modal click hides the modal via delegated listener, edit-feed-form submit delegates to saveFeed sending PUT /api/feeds/{id}.

{"notes":"## r4e.1 & r4e.2: editFeed/createEditFeedModal and refreshFeed tests
- createEditFeedModal appends to document.body, so no DOM setup needed in beforeEach — but the modal persists across tests since beforeEach clears body.innerHTML
- editFeed internally calls createEditFeedModal, so testing editFeed also implicitly tests modal creation
- refreshFeed fake timer tests need careful sequencing: await promise first, then advance timers for the restore setTimeout. The pattern is: call function -> advance 1s for polling -> await promise -> assert intermediate state -> advance 2s for restore -> assert final state
- The catch path test is simpler because the error is synchronous (fetch rejects immediately), no polling loop to advance through
## r4e.5 & r4e.6: Side-effect assertions and setTimeout elimination
- Mocking views.js and feed-errors.js at file level works cleanly since feeds.js imports them directly. Pattern: vi.mock('./views.js', () => ({ applyDefaultViewForScope: vi.fn() }))
- vi.waitFor with { interval: 1 } is the right replacement for setTimeout sleeps when waiting for async event handlers. Default 50ms interval defeats the purpose.
- The initAddFeedForm submit handler is async but attached via addEventListener, so we can't await it directly. vi.waitFor polling on the terminal side-effect (reloadMock or showToast) is the clean pattern.
- showFeedErrorBanner test was previously checking DOM output; now checks the mock call since feed-errors.js is mocked. This is more precise and doesn't depend on feed-errors.js implementation details.
## r4e.3 & r4e.4: saveFeed sidebar/header and initFeedActionListeners delegations
- saveFeed sidebar test: append a sidebar element with .feed-item[href="/feed/{id}"] .feed-name to the existing beforeEach DOM; the function uses an attribute selector on href so the exact format matters.
- saveFeed header/title test: requires Object.defineProperty to mock window.location.pathname since happy-dom doesn't support direct assignment. The pathname comparison in saveFeed is strict equality with the template literal.
- initFeedActionListeners close-edit-modal: straightforward click delegation test, just need the modal with display:flex and a child with data-action.
- initFeedActionListeners form-submit: uses Event('submit', { bubbles: true }) since the delegation is on document. The form needs all edit-feed-* inputs to avoid null reference errors in saveFeed."}

## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. ## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. ## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed."}

Added tests for: null modal guard, default focus behavior, safe closeModal no-op, Tab/Shift+Tab non-boundary behavior, trapFocus without .modal-content, empty modal, Escape preventDefault, and unhandled key passthrough. Tests went from 7 to 16.

Audited read-button.test.js. Found 5 test gaps, all fixed directly (no sub-epic). Added tests for: aria-label assertions for both states, toggle back-and-forth behavior, and falsy/truthy isRead coercion (0/1). Tests went from 5 to 10.

{"notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive."}

old_value:
{"id":"feedreader-7dq.25","title":"Refactor _isStandalone from const to function for testability","description":"**Problem:**
`_isStandalone` in `offline.js` is a module-level `const` evaluated once at import time. In happy-dom, `matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)')` always returns `false`, so the value is permanently `false` after import. This makes three functions untestable past their early-return guard:
- `initOfflineSupport()`
- `handleOnlineStateChange()`
- `updateQueueCacheIfStandalone()`

**Fix:**
Convert `_isStandalone` from a constant to a function `isStandalone()`:
```js
// Before
export const _isStandalone = (typeof window.matchMedia === 'function' &&
    window.matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)').matches) ||
    window.navigator.standalone === true;

// After
export function isStandalone() {
    return (typeof window.matchMedia === 'function' &&
        window.matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)').matches) ||
        window.navigator.standalone === true;
}
```

Update the three call sites in `offline.js` to call `isStandalone()` instead of reading the const.

**Tests:**
- Update `offline.test.js` to remove the existing `_isStandalone` constant tests
- Use `vi.spyOn(offlineModule, 'isStandalone').mockReturnValue(true)` to test the standalone code paths in `initOfflineSupport`, `handleOnlineStateChange`, and `updateQueueCacheIfStandalone`
- Cover: SW ready flow, online/offline transitions with banner + UI disable/enable, queue cache updates

**Scope:** 4 lines changed in `offline.js`, plus test additions in `offline.test.js`. No other modules import `_isStandalone`.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T15:46:54Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T15:46:54Z"}

Added comprehensive tests for standalone code paths: initOfflineSupport (SW ready, offline state, message handler, no-active-worker), handleOnlineStateChange (offline/online transitions with banner, UI state, replay), updateQueueCacheIfStandalone (cache flow, no-active-worker). Tests went from 44 to 56.

Tests went from 38 to 75.

{"id":"feedreader-64p","title":"Fix article card visibly hiding before navigation when Hide Read Articles is enabled","description":"**Problem:**
With Hide Read Articles enabled, clicking an article to read it causes a visible flash: the article card gets the `.read` class styling, then gets hidden (`display: none`), and only then does the page navigate to `/article/{id}`. The user sees the card disappear before the article page loads.

**Expected behavior:**
Clicking an article should navigate immediately. The read status should be synced in the background without any visible DOM changes on the current page.

**Root cause:**
`openArticle()` in `article-actions.js` calls three things in sequence:
1. `markReadSilent(id)` — adds `.read` class to the card (visible styling change)
2. `flushMarkReadQueue()` — fires `api('POST', '/api/articles/batch-read')` (async, non-blocking)
3. `window.location = '/article/{id}'` — starts navigation

The API call from step 2 resolves fast (local server), its `.then()` calls `updateCounts()`, which calls `applyUserPreferences()`, which runs `applyHideReadArticles()` — setting `display: none` on all `.article-card.read` elements. This DOM mutation is visible because navigation has not yet replaced the page.

**Regression history:**
The original implementation (commit ff510f3) only called `markReadSilent(id)` which queued a 250ms debounced flush. Navigation happened before the flush fired, so the user never saw the card hidden. The explicit `flushMarkReadQueue()` call was added during ES modules extraction (commit 5291257), which made the API call fire immediately and its completion side-effects race with navigation.

**Fix options:**
1. **Navigate first, flush after** — Set `window.location` before calling `flushMarkReadQueue()`. The API call will still fire; the browser sends it even during navigation. The `.then()` callback modifying the DOM is harmless since the page is being replaced.
2. **Use `navigator.sendBeacon()` / `keepalive` fetch** — Fire the batch-read API call with `keepalive: true` so it survives navigation, and navigate immediately without waiting.
3. **Skip DOM updates in flushMarkReadQueue when navigating** — Add a guard so `updateCounts()` / `applyUserPreferences()` are not called when navigation is pending.

Option 1 is the simplest and matches the original behavior. The key change is reordering `openArticle()` to:
```js
export function openArticle(id) {
    markReadSilent(id);
    window.location = '/article/{id}';
    flushMarkReadQueue();
}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T16:46:17Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T16:46:17Z"}

new_value: Reordered openArticle() to navigate (window.location) before flushMarkReadQueue(). The API call still fires during navigation but the DOM-modifying .then() callback is harmless since the page is being replaced. This matches the original pre-ES-modules behavior.
