{"notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed."}

Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed."}

{"notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. ## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text)."}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.1","title":"Client sends URL as feed name, preventing server-side Steam name auto-resolution","description":"When adding an RSS feed without typing a name, the client JS sends the URL as the name:

```js
const feedName = ((feedType === 'huggingface' || feedType === 'reddit') && !name) ? '' : (name || url);
```

The `(name || url)` fallback means the server always receives a non-empty name. The server's auto-naming logic (which resolves Steam app names, Reddit subreddit names, etc.) only triggers when `req.Name == ""`, so it's bypassed.

Feed 656 in production shows this: its name is the raw URL `https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/3110760` instead of the resolved game name.

**Fix:** Remove the `feedType` guard so empty names are sent as empty for all feed types, letting the server auto-resolve:

```js
const feedName = name || '';
```

The server already falls back to using the URL as the name when auto-resolution fails, so this is safe for all feed types.

**Files:** `srv/static/modules/feeds.js` (initAddFeedForm)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:25:37Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:25:37Z"}

new_value: Simplified feedName assignment in feeds.js from `((feedType === 'huggingface' || feedType === 'reddit') && !name) ? '' : (name || url)` to `name || ''`. This ensures all feed types send empty name when none is provided, enabling server-side auto-resolution for Steam, Reddit, HuggingFace, and any future feed types.

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.26","title":"Audit test suite for over-mocking and evaluate fake alternatives","description":"Review the JS and Go test suites for problems caused by excessive mocking:

1. **Implementation coupling** — Tests that break on refactoring because they assert on internal calls rather than observable behavior. Look for tests where mock setup dominates and assertions are thin.

2. **Mock drift** — Mocks returning data that does not match what the real dependency actually produces (missing fields, wrong types, absent error cases). Compare mock return values against actual API responses and DOM structures.

3. **Suppressed errors** — Mocks on console.error, console.warn, or error handlers that hide real failures (overlaps with feedreader-cnt but broader scope).

4. **Over-isolation** — Places where multiple units are individually mocked but never tested together, meaning integration bugs go undetected.

5. **Fake candidates** — Identify cases where a fake (lightweight real implementation) would be better than a mock:
   - The `api()` module is mocked in nearly every test file. Consider a fake API layer that returns test fixtures and validates request shapes.
   - DOM setup patterns repeated across test files could become shared test utilities.
   - Any dependency mocked with complex conditional logic is a sign it should be a fake instead.

Produce a summary of findings with specific files and patterns. Create follow-up tasks for actionable improvements. Priority for follow-up tasks should be P3.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T16:29:52Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T16:29:52Z"}

new_value: Completed comprehensive audit of the JS test suite for over-mocking patterns. Key findings: (1) Massive mock duplication: toast.js mocked 13x identically, api.js 7x, plus modal/pagination/feed-errors/counts 3x each. (2) Three incompatible fetch mocking strategies across 17 files. (3) Stateful mock reimplementation in settings-page.test.js. (4) Worst mock-to-test ratio in spa-nav.test.js (6 mocks, 4 tests, 4 assertions). (5) ~100 inline fetch response shapes hardcoded across codebase. (6) Go tests are healthy with minimal mocking. Created 5 follow-up tasks: feedreader-7dq.27 (shared mock helpers), feedreader-7dq.28 (standardize fetch mocking), feedreader-7dq.29 (response fixture factories), feedreader-7dq.30 (replace stateful settings mock), feedreader-7dq.31 (reduce spa-nav mock count).

## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).
## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements."}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.27","title":"Create shared test mock helpers to eliminate vi.mock() duplication","description":"## Problem
13 test files have identical `vi.mock('./toast.js', () => ({ showToast: vi.fn() }))` calls.
7 test files have identical `vi.mock('./api.js', () => ({ api: vi.fn() }))` calls.
modal.js, pagination.js, feed-errors.js, and counts.js are each mocked 3x identically.

This creates maintenance burden — if a module adds an export, all mock sites need updating.

## Solution
Create `srv/static/modules/__test-helpers__/mocks.js` (or similar) with shared mock factory functions. Vitest supports `vi.mock()` with factory functions that can be called from a setup file.

However, note that `vi.mock()` is hoisted to the top of the file by Vitest's transform, so a simple helper function won't work directly. The recommended approach is:

1. Create a `vitest.setup.js` that registers common automocks, OR
2. Use `__mocks__/` directories (Vitest convention) for automatic mocking, OR
3. Create fixture/factory helpers for the *data* (response shapes) while keeping `vi.mock()` calls per-file but with shared mock implementations imported from a helper.

The data factory approach (option 3) is safest and most incremental:
- `makeCountsResponse({unread: 5})` — builds a full counts API response with defaults
- `makeFetchResponse(data)` — wraps data in `{ok: true, json: () => Promise.resolve(data)}`
- `makeArticle({title: 'Test'})` — builds article fixture with defaults

Start with the highest-value targets: toast (13x), api (7x), and the fetch response shape (~100 inline mocks).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-04T00:05:25Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T00:05:25Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"exe.dev user","status":"in_progress"}

Created __mocks__/ directory with 6 shared auto-mock files (toast, api, modal, pagination, feed-errors, counts). Replaced 30+ inline vi.mock() factory functions across 16 test files with simple vi.mock() calls. Net -65 lines of mock boilerplate, all 792 tests pass.

Replaced stateful settings.js mock in settings-page.test.js with real getSetting function using importOriginal. Tests now set window.__settings directly instead of manipulating a mock store. saveSetting and applyHide* remain mocked since they have network/DOM side effects. All 36 settings-page tests pass.

The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).
## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner."}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.28","title":"Standardize fetch mocking strategy across JS tests","description":"## Problem
The test suite uses three incompatible strategies to mock HTTP calls:

1. `vi.mock('./api.js')` — module replacement (7 files: alerts, drag-drop, offline, queue, scraper-page, settings-page, spa-nav)
2. `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')` — spy on global (7 files: api, counts, feeds, folders, opml, search, settings)
3. `window.fetch = vi.fn()` — direct assignment (3 files: article-actions, articles, pagination)

### Risk
- Strategy 1 replaces api.js entirely, so changes to api.js error handling/headers are invisible.
- Strategy 2 tests through the real api.js, better for integration but response shape drift is a risk.
- Strategy 3 doesn't auto-restore, can leak between tests, and is the most fragile.

### Mixed strategies within files
article-actions.test.js uses BOTH `window.fetch = vi.fn()` AND `vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)` in different tests. feeds.test.js also mixes `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')` and `vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)`.

## Solution
1. Decide on a standard approach:
   - For modules that import `api()`: mock `./api.js` (strategy 1) — tests the module's logic, not HTTP
   - For `api.test.js` itself: spy on fetch (strategy 2) — tests the real HTTP layer
   - Eliminate strategy 3 (`window.fetch =`) entirely — replace with `vi.spyOn` or `vi.stubGlobal`

2. Replace all `window.fetch = vi.fn()` with `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')` which auto-restores in afterEach.

3. Document the convention in a test README or vitest.setup.js comment.

### Files to update
- article-actions.test.js: 20+ `window.fetch = vi.fn()` → `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')`
- articles.test.js: 6 `window.fetch = vi.fn()` → `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')`
- pagination.test.js: 10 `window.fetch = vi.fn()` → `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')`","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-04T00:05:41Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T00:05:41Z"}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.32","title":"Replace vi.waitFor polling with direct await in scraper-page.test.js","description":"**Problem:**
`scraper-page.test.js` (54 tests, ~740ms) is the slowest test file — ~10x slower than typical. The dominant cause is **23 `vi.waitFor()` calls** using default settings (50ms polling interval, 1000ms timeout). Each poll cycle adds ~50ms of overhead even when the condition resolves immediately. Tests with multiple sequential `vi.waitFor()` calls (e.g., initAiForm success has 3) take 100ms+ each.

**Root cause:**
Functions like `initScraperPage()` and `checkAiStatus()` fire async operations without returning a promise, so tests must poll the DOM to observe side effects.

**Fix approach:**
1. Refactor async init functions to return the promise chain so tests can `await` them directly.
2. Where that is not feasible, use `await flushPromises()` (a zero-delay microtask flush) instead of polling.
3. As a minimal fix, at least set `{ interval: 1 }` on the `vi.waitFor()` calls to reduce poll overhead from 50ms to 1ms per cycle.

**Expected result:**
Test time should drop from ~740ms to under 100ms (in line with similar-sized test files).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-04T00:20:22Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T00:20:22Z"}

new_value: Replaced all window.fetch = vi.fn() (strategy 3) with vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch') across article-actions.test.js (19 replacements), articles.test.js (6 replacements), pagination.test.js (13 replacements). Also replaced vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...) in article-actions.test.js (3) and feeds.test.js (1). Changed all window.fetch assertion references to globalThis.fetch. Added TESTING.md documenting the convention.

Replaced all 23 vi.waitFor() polling calls with flushPromises() (a zero-delay setTimeout promise). Test runtime dropped from 756ms to ~91ms (8.3x improvement). The flushPromises pattern works because all async operations use mocked api() that resolves immediately — a single macrotask flush settles the entire promise chain.

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms."}

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.33","title":"Reduce pagination.test.js runtime: real timer delay and heavy DOM test","description":"**Problem:**
`pagination.test.js` (39 tests, ~685ms) is the second slowest test file. Two issues:

1. **Real 50ms sleep** — The `checkScrollForMore` test (line ~460) calls `vi.useRealTimers()` then `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50))` for a real wall-clock delay. This should use fake timers with `vi.advanceTimersByTime()` instead. (~50ms waste)

2. **50-element DOM rendering in PAGE_SIZE test** — The `does not set done when articles.length equals PAGE_SIZE` test (line ~259) takes ~366ms alone. It generates 50 article cards via `buildArticleCardHtml()`, sets innerHTML, then `processEmbeds()` runs querySelectorAll on each. This is a heavy DOM operation in happy-dom.

**Fix approach:**
1. Replace the real timer with fake timers + `vi.advanceTimersByTime(50)`.
2. For the PAGE_SIZE test, reduce the test data size: instead of creating PAGE_SIZE (50) full article objects, either:
   - Mock `buildArticleCardHtml` to return minimal HTML (the test only checks `.article-card` count and `done` state, not rendering fidelity)
   - Or use a smaller constant and verify the length-vs-PAGE_SIZE comparison logic, not the actual DOM rendering
3. The 15 repeated `Object.defineProperty(window, 'location', ...)` calls per test add minor overhead — consider a shared helper.

**Expected result:**
Test time should drop from ~685ms to under 150ms.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-04T00:20:37Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T00:20:37Z"}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.34","title":"Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories into __mocks__ auto-mocks","description":"**Context:**
Commit 0a025ac created `__mocks__/` auto-mock files for the 6 most-mocked modules (toast, api, modal, pagination, counts, feed-errors) and removed 30+ inline factory functions. However, 12 inline `vi.mock()` factory calls remain across 8 test files for 7 additional modules.

**Remaining inline factories (by duplication count):**
- `articles.js` — 3 files (counts, search, spa-nav), each mocking different exports
- `views.js` — 2 files (feeds, spa-nav)
- `settings.js` — 2 files (settings-page, views)
- `offline.js` — 2 files (article-actions, queue), identical factories
- `read-button.js` — 1 file (article-actions)
- `sidebar.js` — 1 file (spa-nav)
- `api.js` — 1 file (spa-nav, needs default resolved promise)

**Fix approach:**
1. Create `__mocks__/` files for modules with 2+ duplicated factories: `articles.js`, `views.js`, `settings.js`, `offline.js`. Each auto-mock should export `vi.fn()` stubs for all commonly-mocked exports from the real module.
2. Replace inline factories with bare `vi.mock()` calls where the auto-mock covers all needed exports.
3. For `spa-nav.test.js`'s api.js mock (needs `api` to return a default resolved promise), keep the inline factory or set the return value in `beforeEach` after using the auto-mock.
4. For single-use factories (`read-button.js`, `sidebar.js`), create auto-mocks only if the export surface is simple; otherwise leave inline.

**Also check for duplicated non-module mocks:**
- `MockIntersectionObserver` is duplicated in `article-actions.test.js` and `articles.test.js` — extract to a shared test utility.

**Verification:** `make check` passes, no test regressions.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:53:12Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T01:53:12Z"}

new_value: Reduced pagination.test.js runtime from ~411ms to ~61ms. Fixed both identified bottlenecks: (1) replaced real 50ms timer in checkScrollForMore test with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0) (51ms→1ms), (2) mocked buildArticleCardHtml for PAGE_SIZE test via articles.js auto-mock to avoid rendering 50 full article cards in happy-dom (299ms→3ms). Also mocked processEmbeds as no-op since no test needed its real behavior.

Created 6 new auto-mock files (__mocks__/articles.js, views.js, offline.js, settings.js, read-button.js, sidebar.js) and replaced 10 inline vi.mock() factories across 7 test files with bare vi.mock() calls. Extracted duplicated MockIntersectionObserver into shared test-helpers.js. Only 2 justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (uses importOriginal for real getSetting) and views.test.js (stateful store mock). Updated TESTING.md with auto-mock inventory and shared helpers documentation.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms.
## feedreader-7dq.33: Reduce pagination.test.js runtime
Key bottleneck was the PAGE_SIZE test rendering 50 full articles via buildArticleCardHtml + processEmbeds in happy-dom (~300ms). Mocking buildArticleCardHtml to return minimal HTML dropped it to 3ms. The other bottleneck (real 50ms setTimeout) was fixed with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0). Total: 411ms → 61ms. Approach: mock articles.js via auto-mock at module level, configure buildArticleCardHtml default in beforeEach to return HTML with article-card and article-content-preview classes, then override to minimal HTML in the PAGE_SIZE test.

## feedreader-7dq.34: Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories
Created 6 new auto-mocks. Key insight: ESM destructured imports (import { fn } from './mod.js') in source code DO get intercepted by vi.mock() because Vitest makes the module namespace mutable — the import binding is a live reference to the mock. So auto-mocks work correctly for intercepting calls in dependent modules. Two justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (needs importOriginal for real getSetting), views.test.js (stateful store mock). The views.test.js mock could potentially be replaced by using real settings.js with localStorage (same approach as settings-page task .30), but was out of scope."}

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js

## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. ## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.31","title":"Reduce mock count in spa-nav.test.js or split spa-nav module","description":"## Problem
spa-nav.test.js has the worst mock-to-test ratio in the suite:
- 6 vi.mock() calls (api, articles, sidebar, views, feed-errors, counts)
- Only 4 tests with 4 assertions (1.0 expects/test)
- 30 lines of mock setup for 70 lines of actual test code

This suggests either:
1. spa-nav.js has too many responsibilities (orchestrator coupling), or
2. The tests should be more integrative (use fewer mocks, test the integration)

## Solution
Evaluate two approaches:

**Option A: More integrative tests**
- Allow some real modules to execute (e.g., sidebar, views) since they do DOM manipulation that's easy to verify
- Only mock api.js (the network boundary) and counts.js (which also calls the network)
- This would catch integration bugs between spa-nav and its dependencies

**Option B: Refactor spa-nav.js**
- If spa-nav.js is purely a wiring module (connecting navigation events to other modules), it may not need individual unit tests at all
- Instead, test the navigation behavior in an integration-style test that uses real modules

Review spa-nav.js to determine which approach fits better.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-04T00:06:19Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T00:06:19Z"}

Documented in TESTING.md.

Chose Option A (more integrative tests). Kept 6 auto-mocks but made each earn its keep: expanded from 4 tests/4 assertions to 16 tests with assertions on all 6 mocked modules. Added full popstate/restoreFromState coverage (restore unread/starred, non-article-page reload, unknown-route reload, API error handling, dropdown/button cleanup), route filtering (feed/category clicks not intercepted), and history state management. Mock-to-test ratio went from 1.5:1 to 0.375:1.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms.
## feedreader-7dq.33: Reduce pagination.test.js runtime
Key bottleneck was the PAGE_SIZE test rendering 50 full articles via buildArticleCardHtml + processEmbeds in happy-dom (~300ms). Mocking buildArticleCardHtml to return minimal HTML dropped it to 3ms. The other bottleneck (real 50ms setTimeout) was fixed with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0). Total: 411ms → 61ms. Approach: mock articles.js via auto-mock at module level, configure buildArticleCardHtml default in beforeEach to return HTML with article-card and article-content-preview classes, then override to minimal HTML in the PAGE_SIZE test.

## feedreader-7dq.34: Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories
Created 6 new auto-mocks. Key insight: ESM destructured imports (import { fn } from './mod.js') in source code DO get intercepted by vi.mock() because Vitest makes the module namespace mutable — the import binding is a live reference to the mock. So auto-mocks work correctly for intercepting calls in dependent modules. Two justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (needs importOriginal for real getSetting), views.test.js (stateful store mock). The views.test.js mock could potentially be replaced by using real settings.js with localStorage (same approach as settings-page task .30), but was out of scope.
## feedreader-7dq.29: API response fixture factories
Created 3 factories in test-helpers.js: makeFetchResponse(data, {ok, status}), makeCountsResponse(overrides), makeArticle(overrides). The makeFetchResponse includes both json() and text() methods on the fake Response, which is important since some error paths call .text() instead of .json(). For counts.test.js, a local mockCountsFetch() wrapper that composes both factories eliminated all 13 inline response shapes with a single function call per test.

## feedreader-7dq.31: spa-nav mock reduction
Option A (more integrative tests) was the right call. The module is a genuine orchestrator — it *should* call 6 different modules during navigation. Reducing mocks would mean testing DOM manipulation of real sidebar/views/counts modules, which adds coupling without real benefit. Instead, making each mock earn its assertions revealed that the test file was missing coverage for: popstate handling (reload on non-article pages, restore unread/starred), error handling (both navigateToNavRoute and restoreFromState catch blocks), route filtering (feed/category clicks correctly ignored), and DOM side effects (dropdown clearing, feed-action button hiding). The restoreFromState feed/category paths use dynamic import('./feeds.js') which is harder to test in isolation — would need vi.mock with dynamic import support."}
