{"id":"feedreader-7dq","title":"Improve test quality and coverage","description":"Parent epic for all test improvement work: coverage, performance, and correctness of the JS test suite.","acceptance_criteria":"All child epics closed.","notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).
## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

{"notes":"## feedreader-maz: Import map ordering fix
- Simple one-line swap in base.html: importmap before modulepreload
- Per HTML spec, import maps must be processed before any module loading begins
## feedreader-lx2: AbortController cleanup
All 13 modules done. Key gotchas:
- Modules with _reset*State() must abort the AC there too (articles, scraper-page, pagination)
- Tests that call init at describe scope break when reset aborts the AC — move init into beforeEach
- capture:true listeners need { capture: true, signal } (not just true as 3rd arg)
- ESLint no-use-before-define warns when reset fn references AC declared later — pre-existing pattern from article-actions.js, warnings only
- 8 pre-existing eslint warnings about use-before-define for AC variables in reset functions (articles, pagination, article-actions)"}

new_value: Starred handler now uses articlePageSize constant (50) instead of hardcoded 50. Cursor-based pagination already worked via JS initPagination + /api/articles/starred API endpoint, so this was primarily a consistency fix.

Queue API now supports cursor-based pagination via after_time/after_id query params. Without cursor, returns full list up to queueMaxArticles (500, up from 200). Added ListQueueArticlesCursor SQL query and parseAfterCursor helper. Queue HTML handler uses named constant.

{"notes":"## feedreader-e2l.7 & e2l.8: Starred & Queue pagination
- Starred view already had working infinite-scroll pagination via JS (initPagination maps /starred to /api/articles/starred which supports cursors). Fix was just using the articlePageSize constant.
- Queue view has fundamentally different UI (one article at a time with Next button). Queue JS loads all IDs into a JSON blob. Added cursor support to the API for consistency but the HTML page still loads up to queueMaxArticles (500) since the UI needs all IDs.
- Added parseAfterCursor() for ASC-ordered cursor parsing (queue sorts oldest-first, unlike other views)."}

{"acceptance_criteria":"1. YouTube appears as a feed type in the dropdown
2. User can paste a playlist URL and it creates a working RSS feed
3. User can paste a channel URL and it creates a working RSS feed
4. User can paste a @handle URL and it creates a working RSS feed
5. Feed is auto-named from the YouTube feed title
6. Existing YouTube Atom parsing (embeds, thumbnails) works unchanged","description":"Add YouTube as a feed type in the dropdown (like Reddit and HuggingFace), with client-side URL construction and server-side auto-naming.

**Client-side (feeds.js):**
- New 'youtube' option in the feed type dropdown
- Fields: URL input (accepts playlist URL, channel URL, or @handle URL)
- Client extracts playlist ID or channel ID and constructs the Atom feed URL: `https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?playlist_id=PLxxx` or `?channel_id=UCxxx`
- For @handle URLs, may need server-side resolution (the handle→channel ID mapping isn't in the URL)
- Sends `feedType: 'rss'` on the wire (like Reddit does), since the Atom parser already handles YouTube

**Server-side (feed source registry):**
- Register a YouTube source in the new registry from feedreader-e2d.3.5
- ResolveName: fetch the feed XML and extract the <title> element
- Match: detect youtube.com/youtu.be URLs with feedType 'youtube'

**Templates (feeds.html):**
- YouTube-specific form fields (matching the Reddit/HuggingFace pattern)

The existing Atom parser already handles YouTube entries (media:group, yt:video GUIDs, embeds, thumbnails) — no parser changes needed.","title":"Add YouTube feed type to UI and server"}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.1","title":"Auto-convert YouTube URLs to feed URLs","description":"In apiCreateFeed, detect YouTube URLs and convert them to Atom feed URLs:
- `youtube.com/playlist?list=PLxxx` or `youtu.be/playlist?list=PLxxx` → `youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?playlist_id=PLxxx`
- `youtube.com/channel/UCxxx` → `youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=UCxxx`  
- `youtube.com/@handle` → resolve to channel ID, then convert (requires fetching the page or using oEmbed)
- Auto-name the feed from the Atom feed's <title> element (similar to Steam/Reddit auto-naming)

Follow the existing patterns: convertSteamNewsURL for URL conversion, auto-name block for name resolution. The existing Atom parser already handles YouTube entries correctly (media:group, yt:video GUIDs, embeds, thumbnails) so no parser changes needed.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Pasting a youtube.com/playlist?list=PLxxx URL creates a working feed
2. Pasting a youtube.com/channel/UCxxx URL creates a working feed
3. Pasting a youtube.com/@handle URL creates a working feed
4. Feed is auto-named from the YouTube feed title
5. Existing YouTube Atom parsing (embeds, thumbnails) works unchanged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:13:46Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:13:46Z"}

{"description":"Wire the YouTube Data API client into the feed fetcher to support playlists where new videos are appended to the end (past position 15, invisible to RSS).

Implementation:
- When a YouTube playlist feed is created with 'add to bottom' order, store as feed_type `youtube-playlist` instead of `rss`
- Add `youtube-playlist` case to the fetcher switch statement
- Store playlist config in scraper_config JSON: `{"playlist_id": "PLxxx"}`
- On each fetch, use the Data API to paginate to the end of the playlist and find newly added items
- Track `last_known_count` in scraper_config to avoid re-fetching everything each poll
- Convert API responses to FeedItem and store via CreateArticle upsert (yt:video GUID prevents duplicates)
- If no YouTube API key is configured, fall back to RSS (first 15 items only) and log a warning

**UI changes:**
- Add 'add to top' / 'add to bottom' option in the YouTube feed type form
- 'Add to top' uses plain RSS (feed_type: rss) — works today
- 'Add to bottom' uses Data API (feed_type: youtube-playlist) — requires API key

**User-level API key:**
- Add a `youtube_api_key` user setting (stored in user_settings table)
- Expose in the settings page
- Pass to the fetcher when constructing the YouTube API client"}
old_value:
{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.3","title":"'Add to bottom' playlist support with position tracking","description":"Wire the YouTube Data API client into the feed fetcher to support playlists where new videos are appended to the end (past position 15, invisible to RSS).

Implementation:
- New feed type `youtube-playlist` in the fetcher switch statement
- Store the playlist ID and fetch direction in scraper_config JSON: `{"playlist_id": "PLxxx", "order": "bottom"}`
- On each fetch, use the API to get the last page of playlist items (paginate to the end, or use the total count to jump near the end)
- Track `last_known_count` or `last_seen_position` in scraper_config to avoid re-fetching the entire playlist every time
- Only fetch new items beyond the last known position
- Convert to FeedItem and store via the existing CreateArticle upsert (yt:video GUID prevents duplicates)
- If no YouTube API key is configured, fall back to RSS and log a warning

User-level API key:
- Add a `youtube_api_key` user setting (stored in user_settings table)
- Expose in the settings page UI
- Pass to the fetcher when constructing the YouTube API client","acceptance_criteria":"1. Feed type youtube-playlist fetches new items appended to the end of a playlist
2. Only newly added items are fetched on subsequent polls (not the full playlist)
3. Duplicate videos are handled via GUID upsert
4. Works with user-configured YouTube API key from settings
5. Falls back to RSS gracefully when no API key is set
6. Position tracking persists across fetches","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:10Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:10Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.5","title":"Refactor feed source handling into a registry pattern","description":"Replace the if-then chain in apiCreateFeed with a feed source registry. Currently, URL normalization and auto-naming are scattered across inline if-blocks for Steam, Reddit, and HuggingFace. Adding YouTube would extend this further.

Extract a FeedSource registry where each source declares:
- `Match(url, feedType string) bool` — does this source handle this input?
- `NormalizeURL(ctx, url string) (string, error)` — convert human URL to feed URL (e.g. Steam news → RSS)
- `ResolveName(ctx, url, config string) string` — derive a feed name
- `FeedType() string` — what feed_type to store (e.g. Reddit UI type → "rss" on the wire)

The apiCreateFeed handler iterates the registry instead of branching. Each source is independently testable.

Sources to migrate:
- Steam: NormalizeURL (news→feed conversion) + ResolveName (Steam API name lookup)
- Reddit: ResolveName (regex extract r/name). Note: Reddit URL construction happens client-side, server only auto-names.
- HuggingFace: ResolveName (API call). Note: config-based, feedType stays "huggingface".

This sets up a clean pattern for YouTube and future sources.","acceptance_criteria":"1. apiCreateFeed no longer has feed-source-specific if-blocks — uses registry loop instead
2. Steam, Reddit, and HuggingFace behavior is unchanged
3. Each source is unit-testable in isolation
4. Adding a new source requires only registering a new entry","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:32:56Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:32:56Z"}

new_value: Extracted feed source logic into srv/sources/ package with Registry pattern. Steam, Reddit, and HuggingFace sources implement the Source interface (Match, NormalizeURL, ResolveName, FeedType). apiCreateFeed now calls s.Sources.Resolve() instead of inline if-blocks. All tests migrated and passing. Adding a new source requires only implementing the interface and registering in defaults.go.

{"notes":"## feedreader-e2d.3.5: Feed source registry
- Created srv/sources/ package with Source interface and Registry
- Interface: Match(url, feedType) bool, NormalizeURL(ctx, url), ResolveName(ctx, url, scraperConfig), FeedType()
- Registry.Resolve() does full normalization+naming in one call; returns inputs unchanged if no source matches
- Steam/Reddit/HuggingFace sources migrated with full test coverage
- Server.Sources field initialized via sources.DefaultRegistry() in New() and test helpers
- New sources just need to implement the interface and register in defaults.go
- Tests that used internal steamClient swapping now use sources.SetSteamClient() or package-internal access"}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.2","title":"YouTube Data API integration for playlist fetching","description":"Add a YouTube Data API v3 client for fetching playlist items. This is needed because the RSS feed only returns the first 15 items in playlist order, which misses new additions to 'add to bottom' playlists.

Implementation:
- New package `srv/youtube/` with a client that calls `playlistItems.list` endpoint
- Accepts an API key (no OAuth needed — public playlist data only)
- Supports pagination via pageToken to traverse the full playlist
- Returns items as FeedItem structs compatible with the existing pipeline
- Extract video title, description, thumbnail, published date, channel/author, and video URL
- Handle API errors gracefully (quota exceeded, invalid key, playlist not found)

The YouTube Data API v3 playlistItems.list endpoint:
- `GET https://www.googleapis.com/youtube/v3/playlistItems?part=snippet&playlistId=PLxxx&maxResults=50&pageToken=xxx`
- Returns up to 50 items per page with nextPageToken for pagination
- snippet includes: title, description, thumbnails, videoOwnerChannelTitle, publishedAt, resourceId.videoId","acceptance_criteria":"1. YouTube API client can fetch all items from a playlist via pagination
2. API responses are converted to FeedItem structs with correct GUID, title, URL, thumbnail, content (embed), and published date
3. API errors (quota, invalid key, not found) produce clear error messages
4. Client is tested with mock responses","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:13:58Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:13:58Z"}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.3","title":"'Add to bottom' playlist support with position tracking","description":"Wire the YouTube Data API client into the feed fetcher to support playlists where new videos are appended to the end (past position 15, invisible to RSS).

Implementation:
- When a YouTube playlist feed is created with 'add to bottom' order, store as feed_type `youtube-playlist` instead of `rss`
- Add `youtube-playlist` case to the fetcher switch statement
- Store playlist config in scraper_config JSON: `{"playlist_id": "PLxxx"}`
- On each fetch, use the Data API to paginate to the end of the playlist and find newly added items
- Track `last_known_count` in scraper_config to avoid re-fetching everything each poll
- Convert API responses to FeedItem and store via CreateArticle upsert (yt:video GUID prevents duplicates)
- If no YouTube API key is configured, fall back to RSS (first 15 items only) and log a warning

**UI changes:**
- Add 'add to top' / 'add to bottom' option in the YouTube feed type form
- 'Add to top' uses plain RSS (feed_type: rss) — works today
- 'Add to bottom' uses Data API (feed_type: youtube-playlist) — requires API key

**User-level API key:**
- Add a `youtube_api_key` user setting (stored in user_settings table)
- Expose in the settings page
- Pass to the fetcher when constructing the YouTube API client","acceptance_criteria":"1. Feed type youtube-playlist fetches new items appended to the end of a playlist
2. Only newly added items are fetched on subsequent polls (not the full playlist)
3. Duplicate videos are handled via GUID upsert
4. Works with user-configured YouTube API key from settings
5. Falls back to RSS gracefully when no API key is set
6. Position tracking persists across fetches","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:10Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:33:18Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feedreader-wei","title":"Folder name overflow causes view-toggle to overlap search bar at ~860px","description":"On the folder detail page, folders with long names don't overflow properly at screen widths around 860px. The folder name pushes the view-toggle into the search bar, causing overlap. The folder name should truncate with ellipsis or wrap so the header controls don't collide.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"exe.dev user","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:14:22Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-03T03:07:51Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T03:07:51Z","close_reason":"Added min-width:0, overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, white-space:nowrap to .view-header h1 (base level) and flex-shrink:0 to .view-toggle. Long folder names now truncate with ellipsis instead of pushing the view-toggle into the search bar."}

new_value:
{"notes":"## Replication Notes (2026-03-04)

The original fix (min-width:0, overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, white-space:nowrap on h1 + flex-shrink:0 on .view-toggle) is incomplete. The h1 has no flex or max-width constraint, so it takes its full content width in the flex-wrap container. This creates a **Goldilocks zone** at ~860px viewport where names of ~22-29 mixed-case characters are too wide for the controls to fit on the same line but not wide enough to trigger flex wrapping.

### How to replicate

1. Set browser/viewport to **860px** wide
2. Set up mitm proxy for test user (dev-user-1)
3. Rename feed id=3: `sqlite3 db.sqlite3 "UPDATE feeds SET name = 'simular-ai/Agent-S releases' WHERE id = 3;"`
4. Navigate to http://localhost:3000/feed/3
5. Observe: view toggles are gone, search box crushed to ~80px showing 'Searc'
6. Restore: `sqlite3 db.sqlite3 "UPDATE feeds SET name = 'Lobsters' WHERE id = 3;"`

### Behavior by name length at 860px

| Name | Chars | Result |
|------|-------|--------|
| `Hacker News Front Page` | 22 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `BBC World News Technology` | 25 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `Ars Technica - All Content` | 26 | ❌ Single row, only 1 view toggle visible, search crushed |
| `simular-ai/Agent-S releases` | 27 | ❌ Single row, view toggles gone, search shows 'Searc' |
| `The Verge - Technology News` | 27 | ❌ Single row, view toggles partially visible, search crushed |
| `AI Research Papers Updates!!` | 28 | ❌ Single row, only 1 view toggle sliver visible, search crushed |
| `Machine Learning Weekly Digest` | 30 | ✅ Truncated with ellipsis, wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `AAAAAAAAAA` (wide chars) | 10 | ✅ Single row, all 4 toggles + search visible |
| `AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA` | 25 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |

### Root cause

`.view-header` uses `flex-wrap: wrap` with `gap: 15px`. The h1 has `white-space: nowrap` and `text-overflow: ellipsis` but **no flex-basis, flex-shrink, or max-width constraint**. It expands to its full content width. When the name is in the 26-29 char range (for typical mixed-case text), the h1 + view-toggle + header-actions all try to fit on one row. The h1 'wins' the space, squeezing view-toggle to near-zero width and crushing the search box.

When the name exceeds ~30 chars, the h1 finally triggers flex wrapping and the controls drop to a second row with full width — this is the intended behavior.

### Fix approach

Give the h1 a `flex: 0 1 auto` with a `max-width` that leaves room for the view-toggle, or give view-toggle a `min-width` to prevent it from being crushed to zero. The h1's ellipsis truncation should kick in before the controls lose space."}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq","title":"Improve test quality and coverage","description":"Parent epic for all test improvement work: coverage, performance, and correctness of the JS test suite.","acceptance_criteria":"All child epics closed.","notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).
## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms.
## feedreader-7dq.33: Reduce pagination.test.js runtime
Key bottleneck was the PAGE_SIZE test rendering 50 full articles via buildArticleCardHtml + processEmbeds in happy-dom (~300ms). Mocking buildArticleCardHtml to return minimal HTML dropped it to 3ms. The other bottleneck (real 50ms setTimeout) was fixed with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0). Total: 411ms → 61ms. Approach: mock articles.js via auto-mock at module level, configure buildArticleCardHtml default in beforeEach to return HTML with article-card and article-content-preview classes, then override to minimal HTML in the PAGE_SIZE test.

## feedreader-7dq.34: Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories
Created 6 new auto-mocks. Key insight: ESM destructured imports (import { fn } from './mod.js') in source code DO get intercepted by vi.mock() because Vitest makes the module namespace mutable — the import binding is a live reference to the mock. So auto-mocks work correctly for intercepting calls in dependent modules. Two justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (needs importOriginal for real getSetting), views.test.js (stateful store mock). The views.test.js mock could potentially be replaced by using real settings.js with localStorage (same approach as settings-page task .30), but was out of scope.
## feedreader-7dq.29: API response fixture factories
Created 3 factories in test-helpers.js: makeFetchResponse(data, {ok, status}), makeCountsResponse(overrides), makeArticle(overrides). The makeFetchResponse includes both json() and text() methods on the fake Response, which is important since some error paths call .text() instead of .json(). For counts.test.js, a local mockCountsFetch() wrapper that composes both factories eliminated all 13 inline response shapes with a single function call per test.

## feedreader-7dq.31: spa-nav mock reduction
Option A (more integrative tests) was the right call. The module is a genuine orchestrator — it *should* call 6 different modules during navigation. Reducing mocks would mean testing DOM manipulation of real sidebar/views/counts modules, which adds coupling without real benefit. Instead, making each mock earn its assertions revealed that the test file was missing coverage for: popstate handling (reload on non-article pages, restore unread/starred), error handling (both navigateToNavRoute and restoreFromState catch blocks), route filtering (feed/category clicks correctly ignored), and DOM side effects (dropdown clearing, feed-action button hiding). The restoreFromState feed/category paths use dynamic import('./feeds.js') which is harder to test in isolation — would need vi.mock with dynamic import support.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:40:26Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-04T03:17:56Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T03:17:43Z","close_reason":"all steps complete"}

{"id":"feedreader-wei","title":"Folder name overflow causes view-toggle to overlap search bar at ~860px","description":"On the folder detail page, folders with long names don't overflow properly at screen widths around 860px. The folder name pushes the view-toggle into the search bar, causing overlap. The folder name should truncate with ellipsis or wrap so the header controls don't collide.","notes":"## Replication Notes (2026-03-04)

The original fix (min-width:0, overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, white-space:nowrap on h1 + flex-shrink:0 on .view-toggle) is incomplete. The h1 has no flex or max-width constraint, so it takes its full content width in the flex-wrap container. This creates a **Goldilocks zone** at ~860px viewport where names of ~22-29 mixed-case characters are too wide for the controls to fit on the same line but not wide enough to trigger flex wrapping.

### How to replicate

1. Set browser/viewport to **860px** wide
2. Set up mitm proxy for test user (dev-user-1)
3. Rename feed id=3: `sqlite3 db.sqlite3 "UPDATE feeds SET name = 'simular-ai/Agent-S releases' WHERE id = 3;"`
4. Navigate to http://localhost:3000/feed/3
5. Observe: view toggles are gone, search box crushed to ~80px showing 'Searc'
6. Restore: `sqlite3 db.sqlite3 "UPDATE feeds SET name = 'Lobsters' WHERE id = 3;"`

### Behavior by name length at 860px

| Name | Chars | Result |
|------|-------|--------|
| `Hacker News Front Page` | 22 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `BBC World News Technology` | 25 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `Ars Technica - All Content` | 26 | ❌ Single row, only 1 view toggle visible, search crushed |
| `simular-ai/Agent-S releases` | 27 | ❌ Single row, view toggles gone, search shows 'Searc' |
| `The Verge - Technology News` | 27 | ❌ Single row, view toggles partially visible, search crushed |
| `AI Research Papers Updates!!` | 28 | ❌ Single row, only 1 view toggle sliver visible, search crushed |
| `Machine Learning Weekly Digest` | 30 | ✅ Truncated with ellipsis, wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `AAAAAAAAAA` (wide chars) | 10 | ✅ Single row, all 4 toggles + search visible |
| `AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA` | 25 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |

### Root cause

`.view-header` uses `flex-wrap: wrap` with `gap: 15px`. The h1 has `white-space: nowrap` and `text-overflow: ellipsis` but **no flex-basis, flex-shrink, or max-width constraint**. It expands to its full content width. When the name is in the 26-29 char range (for typical mixed-case text), the h1 + view-toggle + header-actions all try to fit on one row. The h1 'wins' the space, squeezing view-toggle to near-zero width and crushing the search box.

When the name exceeds ~30 chars, the h1 finally triggers flex wrapping and the controls drop to a second row with full width — this is the intended behavior.

### Fix approach

Give the h1 a `flex: 0 1 auto` with a `max-width` that leaves room for the view-toggle, or give view-toggle a `min-width` to prevent it from being crushed to zero. The h1's ellipsis truncation should kick in before the controls lose space.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"exe.dev user","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:14:22Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:27:57Z"}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.4","title":"Retention exemption for YouTube playlist feeds","description":"The retention manager cleans up articles older than 30 days. For YouTube playlist feeds, this is problematic — a user may add a playlist of old videos they intend to watch over time. The published dates will be far in the past and articles could be cleaned up before the user sees them.

Options (pick one during implementation):
1. Exempt youtube-playlist feed type from retention entirely
2. Add a per-feed 'skip retention' flag  
3. Only retain based on fetched_at (not published_at) for playlist feeds

The simplest approach is option 2 — a per-feed boolean that defaults to true for youtube-playlist feeds. This is also useful for other feed types where users want to keep old articles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Articles from YouTube playlist feeds are not deleted by the retention manager
2. The exemption mechanism is general-purpose (not YouTube-specific) so other feeds can opt in","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:19Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:19Z"}

{"design":"## Approach

Work through each test file in `srv/static/modules/*.test.js` and produce a recommendation: **keep** (happy-dom), **migrate** (browser mode), or **split** (some tests migrate, others stay).

Criteria for migration:
1. Test currently uses rendering workarounds or mocks that browser mode would eliminate
2. Test assertions would be more accurate with real layout (e.g. visibility checks)
3. Test runtime stays under 100ms in browser mode (measure and document)

## Configuration

Tests migrated to browser mode can use a `// @vitest-environment browser` directive or be placed in a separate directory with its own config. Prefer the directive to keep tests co-located with their modules."}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.36","title":"Evaluate existing test files for Vitest browser mode migration","description":"With Vitest browser mode infrastructure in place (feedreader-7dq.35), evaluate each existing test file for potential migration from happy-dom to browser mode.

**Goal:** Identify tests that would benefit from real browser rendering accuracy (layout, computed styles, event handling, IntersectionObserver, etc.) without exceeding 100ms runtime per file.

**Not in scope:** Migrating tests that work correctly under happy-dom and don't touch rendering/layout. The default should be to keep tests on happy-dom unless there's a concrete accuracy benefit.

**Candidates likely to benefit:**
- Tests that mock `getBoundingClientRect` or `IntersectionObserver`
- Tests that check computed styles or visibility
- Tests involving scroll behavior or resize observers
- Tests with known happy-dom workarounds (e.g. the DOMExceptions we just fixed)
- Tests that mock `offsetParent`, `offsetWidth`, `offsetHeight`

**Candidates unlikely to benefit (keep on happy-dom):**
- Pure logic tests (utils, api, icons, timestamps)
- Tests that only check DOM structure, not rendering
- Tests where happy-dom is accurate enough and browser mode would just be slower","design":"## Approach

Work through each test file in `srv/static/modules/*.test.js` and produce a recommendation: **keep** (happy-dom), **migrate** (browser mode), or **split** (some tests migrate, others stay).

Criteria for migration:
1. Test currently uses rendering workarounds or mocks that browser mode would eliminate
2. Test assertions would be more accurate with real layout (e.g. visibility checks)
3. Test runtime stays under 100ms in browser mode (measure and document)

For each file, create a child task only if the recommendation is **migrate** or **split**. Files that stay on happy-dom just get documented in the epic notes.

## Configuration

Tests migrated to browser mode can use a `// @vitest-environment browser` directive or be placed in a separate directory with its own config. Prefer the directive to keep tests co-located with their modules.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every test file in `srv/static/modules/*.test.js` has been evaluated and documented
- Migrated tests run correctly in browser mode with ≤100ms runtime per file
- No test accuracy regressions — migrated tests should be at least as reliable as before
- Happy-dom workarounds in migrated tests are removed (cleaner test code)
- Epic notes contain the full evaluation matrix with keep/migrate/split decisions and rationale","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:46:06Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:46:06Z"}

new_value: Added flex: 0 1 auto and max-width: calc(100% - 350px) to .view-header h1. This constrains the h1 width so it truncates with ellipsis before pushing view-toggle and search off-screen. Also added form-group-checkbox CSS class. Verified at 860px (all 4 view toggles visible), 1200px (full title shown), and 768px mobile (existing responsive behavior preserved).

Added skip_retention column to feeds table (migration 017). Retention queries (Delete/Count, per-user and global) now exclude feeds with skip_retention=1. YouTube-playlist feeds auto-set skip_retention=1 on creation. The flag is exposed in the feed edit modal as a 'Skip retention cleanup' checkbox, allowing any feed to opt out of retention. Updated sqlc queries, server handlers, JS modal, and all related tests.

{"notes":"## feedreader-e2d.3.1: YouTube feed type in UI and server
- YouTubeSource registered in sources/defaults.go
- @handle resolution works by scraping the YouTube page HTML for canonical link or channelId meta tag
- FeedType() returns 'rss' — no new DB feed type needed since YouTube Atom feeds work with existing parser
- Client sends raw YouTube URL with feedType='youtube'; all normalization is server-side
- ParseYouTubeURL() exported for potential use by other components
- YouTube Atom feed title extraction uses a lightweight XML decode (64KB limit)
## feedreader-e2d.3.2: YouTube Data API integration\n- Package at srv/youtube/ with Client struct, NewClient(apiKey)\n- GUIDs use 'yt:video:' prefix matching existing YouTube Atom parser convention\n- Content includes iframe embed + HTML-escaped description paragraph\n- Thumbnail selection prefers high > medium > default\n- API base URL is a const (apiBaseURL) for easy testing\n- Tests use a roundTripFunc transport to redirect requests to httptest.Server\n- maxPages=0 means unlimited; useful for the 'add to bottom' task to control how deep we paginate
## feedreader-e2d.3.3: 'Add to bottom' playlist support
- youtube.Item type replaces feeds.FeedItem in the youtube package to avoid import cycle (youtube → feeds → youtube)
- YouTubePlaylistConfig struct lives in feeds package (exported for use by server.go)
- Position tracking uses last_known_count vs totalResults from API; items past last_known_count are new
- Fetcher reads youtubeApiKey from user_settings via GetUserSetting query
- New UpdateFeedScraperConfig query for persisting config changes without touching other feed fields
- playlistOrder 'bottom' triggers youtube-playlist type; 'top' or empty stays RSS
- Playlist detection in UI uses regex /\/playlist\?list=|[?&]playlist_id=/ on the input field
## feedreader-e2d.3.4: Retention exemption for YouTube playlist feeds
- Chose option 2 (per-feed skip_retention boolean) — general-purpose, not YouTube-specific
- Column defaults to 0; auto-set to 1 for youtube-playlist feed type on creation
- All 4 retention queries updated (Delete/Count, per-user and global variants)
- Exposed in edit feed modal so users can toggle it for any feed
- No separate DB query needed — piggybacked on existing CreateFeed and UpdateFeed queries"}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3","title":"YouTube Playlist Feed Support","description":"Support YouTube playlists as feed sources. YouTube exposes playlist Atom feeds at `https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?playlist_id=PLxxx` but they are limited to 15 entries in playlist-position order. This works for 'add to top' playlists (newest first) but breaks for 'add to bottom' playlists where new items append past position 15 and never appear in the feed. Phase 1 uses the existing RSS/Atom parser with URL auto-detection. Phase 2 adds YouTube Data API v3 integration to support 'add to bottom' playlists via paginated playlistItems.list calls.","design":"Two-phase approach:

**Phase 1 — RSS-only (no API key needed):**
- Auto-convert YouTube URLs to feed URLs in apiCreateFeed: `youtube.com/playlist?list=PLxxx` → `youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?playlist_id=PLxxx`, and channel URLs → channel feed URLs
- Auto-name feeds from the Atom feed title
- Existing Atom parser already handles YouTube entries (media:group, embeds, thumbnails, yt:video GUIDs)
- This supports 'add to top' playlists immediately

**Phase 2 — YouTube Data API for 'add to bottom' playlists:**
- New user-level setting for YouTube API key (stored in user_settings)
- New feed type `youtube-playlist` that fetches via Data API v3 playlistItems.list
- Supports pagination to reach the last page of a playlist and find newly appended videos
- Track last-seen playlist position or item count to efficiently detect new additions
- Convert API responses to FeedItem structs matching the existing pipeline
- Falls back to RSS if no API key is configured

Articles from both phases are stored and displayed as normal feed items — no playlist-specific UX.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Pasting a YouTube playlist URL auto-converts to feed URL and fetches successfully
2. Pasting a YouTube channel URL auto-converts to feed URL and fetches successfully
3. Feed auto-names from the YouTube feed title
4. 'Add to top' playlists show new videos via standard RSS polling
5. 'Add to bottom' playlists show new videos via YouTube Data API when API key is configured
6. All YouTube articles display with embedded video player and thumbnail
7. Retention manager does not prematurely delete playlist articles","notes":"## feedreader-e2d.3.1: YouTube feed type in UI and server

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.35.1","title":"Set up Vitest browser mode infrastructure","description":"Install and configure Vitest browser mode with Playwright as the browser provider.

Steps:
- `npm install -D @vitest/browser playwright`
- Create `vitest.browser.config.mjs` with browser mode config (chromium, headless)
- Layout tests in `tests/layout/` directory
- Create `tests/layout/helpers.js` with reusable assertion functions:
  - `overlaps(rectA, rectB)` — true if two DOMRects intersect
  - `assertNoOverlap(page, selectorA, selectorB)` — assert no bounding-rect overlap
  - `assertMinWidth(page, selector, minPx)` — assert element width ≥ N px
  - `assertVisible(page, selector)` — assert non-zero width and height

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.35","title":"Automated layout tests with Vitest browser mode","description":"Add automated layout regression tests using Vitest browser mode (backed by Playwright). These tests run in a real browser to get accurate bounding-rect measurements, detecting element overlap, crushed controls, and broken layouts at various viewport widths.

Initial focus is the .view-header component which has a known Goldilocks-zone bug (feedreader-wei) where feed names of ~26-29 chars at ~860px cause view toggles to disappear and the search box to collapse.

Tests cover three viewport categories:
- **Narrow mobile** (~375-430px) — hamburger menu, stacked layout
- **Tablet/narrow desktop** (~860px) — the known problem zone
- **Wide desktop** (1920px) — full layout with plenty of room","design":"## Infrastructure

- Add `@vitest/browser` and `playwright` as dev dependencies
- Create a separate vitest config (`vitest.browser.config.mjs`) that uses browser mode with Playwright/chromium
- Layout tests live in `tests/layout/` (separate from unit tests in `srv/static/modules/`)
- Add a `make layout-test` target (not part of `make check` since it needs a running server)
- Tests start with the server already running on localhost:3000 (mitm proxy with test user headers)

## Test helpers

- `overlaps(rectA, rectB)` — returns true if two DOMRects intersect
- `assertNoOverlap(selectorA, selectorB)` — get bounding rects, assert no overlap
- `assertMinWidth(selector, minPx)` — element must be at least N pixels wide
- `assertVisible(selector)` — element has non-zero width and height
- `setFeedName(name)` — API call or direct DB update to change feed name for testing
- `resize(width)` — set viewport width

## Test matrix for .view-header

For each viewport width, test with feed names of varying lengths (10, 20, 26, 27, 30, 40, 50 chars) using realistic mixed-case text. Assert:
- `.view-toggle` is visible with width > 0 (or correctly hidden at mobile)
- `#search` / `.search-box` has minimum usable width (≥120px on non-mobile)
- No overlap between h1, .view-toggle, .header-actions
- No overlap between search box and mark-read dropdown
- h1 truncates with ellipsis rather than overflowing","acceptance_criteria":"- Vitest browser mode is configured and documented
- Layout tests run via `make layout-test` against a running server
- Header layout is tested at 375px, 860px, and 1920px with feed names of 10-50 chars
- Tests catch the feedreader-wei Goldilocks-zone bug (would fail on current code at 860px with 27-char name)
- Test helpers are reusable for future layout tests beyond the header","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:40:14Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:40:14Z"}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.35.5","title":"Use Playwright Test instead of Vitest browser mode for layout tests","description":"Vitest browser mode runs tests inside a browser iframe managed by Vitest. Navigating the Playwright page (via page.goto) to the app's URL destroys the Vitest WebSocket connection, killing the test harness. This is a fundamental limitation: Vitest browser mode is designed for testing components rendered in its own iframe, not for testing external pages.

Playwright Test is the natural fit for layout tests because:
1. Tests need to navigate to the real app (localhost:3000) to measure actual layout
2. Playwright provides direct page.goto(), page.setViewportSize(), locator.boundingBox()
3. No iframe indirection — tests operate on the actual page DOM
4. Mature, well-documented API designed for exactly this use case

The layout test infrastructure uses @playwright/test with a separate playwright.config.mjs. The 'make layout-test' target runs these tests. They remain separate from 'make check' since they require a running server.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:12:21Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:13:04Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T06:13:04Z","close_reason":"Decision documented and implemented in feedreader-7dq.35.1"}

{"notes":"## Replication Notes (2026-03-04)

The original fix (min-width:0, overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, white-space:nowrap on h1 + flex-shrink:0 on .view-toggle) is incomplete. The h1 has no flex or max-width constraint, so it takes its full content width in the flex-wrap container. This creates a **Goldilocks zone** at ~860px viewport where names of ~22-29 mixed-case characters are too wide for the controls to fit on the same line but not wide enough to trigger flex wrapping.

### How to replicate

1. Set browser/viewport to **860px** wide
2. Set up mitm proxy for test user (dev-user-1)
3. Rename feed id=3: `sqlite3 db.sqlite3 "UPDATE feeds SET name = 'simular-ai/Agent-S releases' WHERE id = 3;"`
4. Navigate to http://localhost:3000/feed/3
5. Observe: view toggles are gone, search box crushed to ~80px showing 'Searc'
6. Restore: `sqlite3 db.sqlite3 "UPDATE feeds SET name = 'Lobsters' WHERE id = 3;"`

### Behavior by name length at 860px

| Name | Chars | Result |
|------|-------|--------|
| `Hacker News Front Page` | 22 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `BBC World News Technology` | 25 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `Ars Technica - All Content` | 26 | ❌ Single row, only 1 view toggle visible, search crushed |
| `simular-ai/Agent-S releases` | 27 | ❌ Single row, view toggles gone, search shows 'Searc' |
| `The Verge - Technology News` | 27 | ❌ Single row, view toggles partially visible, search crushed |
| `AI Research Papers Updates!!` | 28 | ❌ Single row, only 1 view toggle sliver visible, search crushed |
| `Machine Learning Weekly Digest` | 30 | ✅ Truncated with ellipsis, wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |
| `AAAAAAAAAA` (wide chars) | 10 | ✅ Single row, all 4 toggles + search visible |
| `AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA` | 25 | ✅ Wraps to 2 rows, all controls visible |

### Root cause

`.view-header` uses `flex-wrap: wrap` with `gap: 15px`. The h1 has `white-space: nowrap` and `text-overflow: ellipsis` but **no flex-basis, flex-shrink, or max-width constraint**. It expands to its full content width. When the name is in the 26-29 char range (for typical mixed-case text), the h1 + view-toggle + header-actions all try to fit on one row. The h1 'wins' the space, squeezing view-toggle to near-zero width and crushing the search box.

When the name exceeds ~30 chars, the h1 finally triggers flex wrapping and the controls drop to a second row with full width — this is the intended behavior.

### Fix approach

Give the h1 a `flex: 0 1 auto` with a `max-width` that leaves room for the view-toggle, or give view-toggle a `min-width` to prevent it from being crushed to zero. The h1's ellipsis truncation should kick in before the controls lose space.
## Final fix (session 3)
Root cause was header-actions children overflowing left via justify-content:flex-end, painting on top of view-toggle. Fix: removed flex-wrap from desktop .view-header, made h1 the sole flexible item (flex:1 1 auto, min-width:0), moved mark-as-read dropdown into the three-dot overflow menu at ≤1024px. Overflow menu now rendered for all views (feeds, categories, All Unread)."}

{"notes":"## feedreader-7dq.35.1: Set up layout test infrastructure
- Used Playwright Test instead of Vitest browser mode (see decision feedreader-7dq.35.5)
- Vitest browser mode runs tests in an iframe — page.goto() kills the test harness WebSocket
- @playwright/test provides direct page navigation and bounding-box measurement
- Config: playwright.config.mjs, baseURL http://localhost:3000, chromium-only, headless
- Helper API: overlaps(), assertNoOverlap(page, selA, selB), assertMinWidth(page, sel, px), assertVisible(page, sel)
- All helpers skip silently if elements are missing (graceful degradation)
- Tests require: running server on :8000 + mitm proxy on :3000 with test user headers
- xvfb not needed — Playwright's bundled chromium runs headless natively
## Performance: measureLayout is ~1s per call

Benchmarked the custom command infrastructure. Per-call breakdown:
- newPage(): ~40ms
- setViewportSize(): ~1ms  
- page.goto() + networkidle: ~830ms (85% of cost)
- boundingBox() × N selectors: ~90ms
- Total: ~960ms per call

Current design opens a fresh Playwright page per measureLayout call. A 3-width × 5-name test matrix = 15 calls ≈ 15s. Optimization: reuse a page across measurements — navigate once, then resize viewport and re-measure without full page reload. Could also switch from networkidle to domcontentloaded if layout is ready before all network requests finish."}

- page.goto() + networkidle: ~830ms (85% of cost)
- boundingBox() × N selectors: ~90ms
- Total: ~960ms per call

Current design opens a fresh Playwright page per measureLayout call. A 3-width × 5-name test matrix = 15 calls ≈ 15s. Optimization: reuse a page across measurements — navigate once, then resize viewport and re-measure without full page reload. Could also switch from networkidle to domcontentloaded if layout is ready before all network requests finish.
## feedreader-7dq.35.2/3/4: Header layout tests at all three widths
- Feed rename API requires X-Requested-With header for CSRF — added to setFeedName custom command
- At mobile (375/430px), h1 takes flex: 0 0 100% (own row), so name length doesn't affect toggle/search layout — search stays ~155-210px
- At 860px, search shrinks from 197px (10ch) to 80px (50ch) — the CSS min-width floor. 120px threshold from task spec was too high.
- At 1920px, search also hits 80px floor for names >= 27ch because h1 has flex:1 and grows with content, while .header-actions has flex: 0 1 auto (no grow). The mark-read dropdown (143px), edit, and refresh buttons eat remaining space.
- Observed search-box painting outside header-actions bounds at 1920px with long names (search x < header-actions x by ~9px). This is a pre-existing cosmetic issue — filed separately would be appropriate.
- Test duration: ~27s total for 30 tests (28 header + 2 smoke). Each measureLayout call costs ~830ms (page navigation dominates).
- measureLayoutMultiWidth command added for future optimization (reuse page across widths), not yet used by current tests."}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.35","title":"Automated layout tests with Vitest browser mode","description":"Add automated layout regression tests using Vitest browser mode (backed by Playwright). These tests run in a real browser to get accurate bounding-rect measurements, detecting element overlap, crushed controls, and broken layouts at various viewport widths.

Initial focus is the .view-header component which has a known Goldilocks-zone bug (feedreader-wei) where feed names of ~26-29 chars at ~860px cause view toggles to disappear and the search box to collapse.

Tests cover three viewport categories:
- **Narrow mobile** (~375-430px) — hamburger menu, stacked layout
- **Tablet/narrow desktop** (~860px) — the known problem zone
- **Wide desktop** (1920px) — full layout with plenty of room","design":"## Infrastructure

- Add `@vitest/browser` and `playwright` as dev dependencies
- Create a separate vitest config (`vitest.browser.config.mjs`) that uses browser mode with Playwright/chromium
- Layout tests live in `tests/layout/` (separate from unit tests in `srv/static/modules/`)
- Add a `make layout-test` target (not part of `make check` since it needs a running server)
- Tests start with the server already running on localhost:3000 (mitm proxy with test user headers)

## Test helpers

- `overlaps(rectA, rectB)` — returns true if two DOMRects intersect
- `assertNoOverlap(selectorA, selectorB)` — get bounding rects, assert no overlap
- `assertMinWidth(selector, minPx)` — element must be at least N pixels wide
- `assertVisible(selector)` — element has non-zero width and height
- `setFeedName(name)` — API call or direct DB update to change feed name for testing
- `resize(width)` — set viewport width

## Test matrix for .view-header

For each viewport width, test with feed names of varying lengths (10, 20, 26, 27, 30, 40, 50 chars) using realistic mixed-case text. Assert:
- `.view-toggle` is visible with width > 0 (or correctly hidden at mobile)
- `#search` / `.search-box` has minimum usable width (≥120px on non-mobile)
- No overlap between h1, .view-toggle, .header-actions
- No overlap between search box and mark-read dropdown
- h1 truncates with ellipsis rather than overflowing","acceptance_criteria":"- Vitest browser mode is configured and documented
- Layout tests run via `make layout-test` against a running server
- Header layout is tested at 375px, 860px, and 1920px with feed names of 10-50 chars
- Tests catch the feedreader-wei Goldilocks-zone bug (would fail on current code at 860px with 27-char name)
- Test helpers are reusable for future layout tests beyond the header","notes":"## feedreader-7dq.35.1: Set up layout test infrastructure
- Used Playwright Test instead of Vitest browser mode (see decision feedreader-7dq.35.5)
- Vitest browser mode runs tests in an iframe — page.goto() kills the test harness WebSocket
- @playwright/test provides direct page navigation and bounding-box measurement
- Config: playwright.config.mjs, baseURL http://localhost:3000, chromium-only, headless
- Helper API: overlaps(), assertNoOverlap(page, selA, selB), assertMinWidth(page, sel, px), assertVisible(page, sel)
- All helpers skip silently if elements are missing (graceful degradation)
- Tests require: running server on :8000 + mitm proxy on :3000 with test user headers
- xvfb not needed — Playwright's bundled chromium runs headless natively
## Performance: measureLayout is ~1s per call

Benchmarked the custom command infrastructure. Per-call breakdown:
- newPage(): ~40ms
- setViewportSize(): ~1ms  
- page.goto() + networkidle: ~830ms (85% of cost)
- boundingBox() × N selectors: ~90ms
- Total: ~960ms per call

Current design opens a fresh Playwright page per measureLayout call. A 3-width × 5-name test matrix = 15 calls ≈ 15s. Optimization: reuse a page across measurements — navigate once, then resize viewport and re-measure without full page reload. Could also switch from networkidle to domcontentloaded if layout is ready before all network requests finish.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:40:14Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T07:16:48Z"}

Header tested at 375px, 430px, 860px, and 1920px with 7 feed name lengths (10-50 chars). 30 tests total, all passing. Tests catch the feedreader-wei Goldilocks-zone regression. Helpers include measureLayout, measureLayoutMultiWidth, setFeedName/getFeedName for reuse in future layout tests.
All children completed: YouTube feed type with URL auto-detection, Data API integration, 'add to bottom' playlist support with position tracking, and retention exemption for playlist feeds.

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.36","title":"Evaluate existing test files for Vitest browser mode migration","description":"With Vitest browser mode infrastructure in place (feedreader-7dq.35), evaluate each existing test file for potential migration from happy-dom to browser mode.

**Goal:** Identify tests that would benefit from real browser rendering accuracy (layout, computed styles, event handling, IntersectionObserver, etc.) without exceeding 100ms runtime per file.

**Not in scope:** Migrating tests that work correctly under happy-dom and don't touch rendering/layout. The default should be to keep tests on happy-dom unless there's a concrete accuracy benefit.

**Candidates likely to benefit:**
- Tests that mock `getBoundingClientRect` or `IntersectionObserver`
- Tests that check computed styles or visibility
- Tests involving scroll behavior or resize observers
- Tests with known happy-dom workarounds (e.g. the DOMExceptions we just fixed)
- Tests that mock `offsetParent`, `offsetWidth`, `offsetHeight`

**Candidates unlikely to benefit (keep on happy-dom):**
- Pure logic tests (utils, api, icons, timestamps)
- Tests that only check DOM structure, not rendering
- Tests where happy-dom is accurate enough and browser mode would just be slower","design":"## Approach

Work through each test file in `srv/static/modules/*.test.js` and produce a recommendation: **keep** (happy-dom), **migrate** (browser mode), or **split** (some tests migrate, others stay).

Criteria for migration:
1. Test currently uses rendering workarounds or mocks that browser mode would eliminate
2. Test assertions would be more accurate with real layout (e.g. visibility checks)
3. Test runtime stays under 100ms in browser mode (measure and document)

## Configuration

Tests migrated to browser mode can use a `// @vitest-environment browser` directive or be placed in a separate directory with its own config. Prefer the directive to keep tests co-located with their modules.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every test file in `srv/static/modules/*.test.js` has been evaluated and documented
- Migrated tests run correctly in browser mode with ≤100ms runtime per file
- No test accuracy regressions — migrated tests should be at least as reliable as before
- Happy-dom workarounds in migrated tests are removed (cleaner test code)
- Epic notes contain the full evaluation matrix with keep/migrate/split decisions and rationale","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:46:06Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T05:05:12Z"}
