new_value: Migrated 11 drag-drop tests (getDragAfterElementAmongSiblings, placeholder positioning, drop reorder, drop nesting) from happy-dom with mocked getBoundingClientRect to Vitest browser mode with real Chromium layout. Tests now use real element positions instead of manual rect mocks. Key workarounds: vi.spyOn(globalThis, fetch) instead of vi.mock (which hangs in browser mode), setTimeout tracking to clear showToast timers, never-resolving fetch for nesting success test to prevent location.reload. All 15 browser tests pass in ~1.5s.

Migrated 4 checkScrollForMore tests from happy-dom with mocked offsetHeight/innerHeight/scrollY to Vitest browser mode with real scroll measurements. Tests verify: done state skips load, loading state skips load, tall page (5000px) does not trigger load, short page (100px) triggers load near bottom. Shares browser-unit.browser.test.js with drag-drop tests (single-file approach avoids multi-file process exit hang).

{"notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.
## feedreader-7dq.1: Audit alerts.test.js
Found 15 test gaps, all fixable directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness pattern: every async function with a try/catch error path (console.error + showToast) was missing the error test. Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).
## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms.
## feedreader-7dq.33: Reduce pagination.test.js runtime
Key bottleneck was the PAGE_SIZE test rendering 50 full articles via buildArticleCardHtml + processEmbeds in happy-dom (~300ms). Mocking buildArticleCardHtml to return minimal HTML dropped it to 3ms. The other bottleneck (real 50ms setTimeout) was fixed with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0). Total: 411ms → 61ms. Approach: mock articles.js via auto-mock at module level, configure buildArticleCardHtml default in beforeEach to return HTML with article-card and article-content-preview classes, then override to minimal HTML in the PAGE_SIZE test.

## feedreader-7dq.34: Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories
Created 6 new auto-mocks. Key insight: ESM destructured imports (import { fn } from './mod.js') in source code DO get intercepted by vi.mock() because Vitest makes the module namespace mutable — the import binding is a live reference to the mock. So auto-mocks work correctly for intercepting calls in dependent modules. Two justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (needs importOriginal for real getSetting), views.test.js (stateful store mock). The views.test.js mock could potentially be replaced by using real settings.js with localStorage (same approach as settings-page task .30), but was out of scope.
## feedreader-7dq.29: API response fixture factories
Created 3 factories in test-helpers.js: makeFetchResponse(data, {ok, status}), makeCountsResponse(overrides), makeArticle(overrides). The makeFetchResponse includes both json() and text() methods on the fake Response, which is important since some error paths call .text() instead of .json(). For counts.test.js, a local mockCountsFetch() wrapper that composes both factories eliminated all 13 inline response shapes with a single function call per test.

## feedreader-7dq.31: spa-nav mock reduction
Option A (more integrative tests) was the right call. The module is a genuine orchestrator — it *should* call 6 different modules during navigation. Reducing mocks would mean testing DOM manipulation of real sidebar/views/counts modules, which adds coupling without real benefit. Instead, making each mock earn its assertions revealed that the test file was missing coverage for: popstate handling (reload on non-article pages, restore unread/starred), error handling (both navigateToNavRoute and restoreFromState catch blocks), route filtering (feed/category clicks correctly ignored), and DOM side effects (dropdown clearing, feed-action button hiding). The restoreFromState feed/category paths use dynamic import('./feeds.js') which is harder to test in isolation — would need vi.mock with dynamic import support.
## feedreader-7dq.39 + feedreader-7dq.40: Browser mode migration for drag-drop and pagination
Three critical limitations of Vitest browser mode v4.0.18 discovered:

1. **vi.mock() causes process hangs** — Module mocking in browser mode prevents clean process exit. Workaround: mock at fetch level with vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch').

2. **vi.spyOn on ESM namespaces fails** — 'Cannot spy on export — Module namespace is not configurable in ESM'. Cannot intercept module-internal calls. Workaround: mock lower-level APIs (fetch, console) instead.

3. **Multi-file test execution hangs** — Running multiple browser test files causes process to never exit. Workaround: combine all browser unit tests into a single file.

Additional pattern: showToast() creates real 4-second setTimeout timers in browser mode. Track timer IDs in beforeEach and clearTimeout in afterEach to prevent timers from keeping the process alive.

location.reload() in browser mode actually reloads the iframe, crashing test execution. Use never-resolving fetch promises for success paths that call reload.

All tests consolidated in srv/static/modules/browser-unit.browser.test.js. Config: vitest.browser-unit.config.mjs. Make target: browser-unit-test."}

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. ## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. ## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. **Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms.
## feedreader-7dq.33: Reduce pagination.test.js runtime
Key bottleneck was the PAGE_SIZE test rendering 50 full articles via buildArticleCardHtml + processEmbeds in happy-dom (~300ms). Mocking buildArticleCardHtml to return minimal HTML dropped it to 3ms. The other bottleneck (real 50ms setTimeout) was fixed with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0). Total: 411ms → 61ms. Approach: mock articles.js via auto-mock at module level, configure buildArticleCardHtml default in beforeEach to return HTML with article-card and article-content-preview classes, then override to minimal HTML in the PAGE_SIZE test.

## feedreader-7dq.34: Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories
Created 6 new auto-mocks. Key insight: ESM destructured imports (import { fn } from './mod.js') in source code DO get intercepted by vi.mock() because Vitest makes the module namespace mutable — the import binding is a live reference to the mock. So auto-mocks work correctly for intercepting calls in dependent modules. Two justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (needs importOriginal for real getSetting), views.test.js (stateful store mock). The views.test.js mock could potentially be replaced by using real settings.js with localStorage (same approach as settings-page task .30), but was out of scope.
## feedreader-7dq.29: API response fixture factories
Created 3 factories in test-helpers.js: makeFetchResponse(data, {ok, status}), makeCountsResponse(overrides), makeArticle(overrides). The makeFetchResponse includes both json() and text() methods on the fake Response, which is important since some error paths call .text() instead of .json(). For counts.test.js, a local mockCountsFetch() wrapper that composes both factories eliminated all 13 inline response shapes with a single function call per test.

## feedreader-7dq.31: spa-nav mock reduction
Option A (more integrative tests) was the right call. The module is a genuine orchestrator — it *should* call 6 different modules during navigation. Reducing mocks would mean testing DOM manipulation of real sidebar/views/counts modules, which adds coupling without real benefit. Instead, making each mock earn its assertions revealed that the test file was missing coverage for: popstate handling (reload on non-article pages, restore unread/starred), error handling (both navigateToNavRoute and restoreFromState catch blocks), route filtering (feed/category clicks correctly ignored), and DOM side effects (dropdown clearing, feed-action button hiding). The restoreFromState feed/category paths use dynamic import('./feeds.js') which is harder to test in isolation — would need vi.mock with dynamic import support.
## feedreader-7dq.39 + feedreader-7dq.40: Browser mode migration for drag-drop and pagination
Three critical limitations of Vitest browser mode v4.0.18 discovered:

1. **vi.mock() causes process hangs** — Module mocking in browser mode prevents clean process exit. Workaround: mock at fetch level with vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch').

2. **vi.spyOn on ESM namespaces fails** — 'Cannot spy on export — Module namespace is not configurable in ESM'. Cannot intercept module-internal calls. Workaround: mock lower-level APIs (fetch, console) instead.

3. **Multi-file test execution hangs** — Running multiple browser test files causes process to never exit. Workaround: combine all browser unit tests into a single file.

Additional pattern: showToast() creates real 4-second setTimeout timers in browser mode. Track timer IDs in beforeEach and clearTimeout in afterEach to prevent timers from keeping the process alive.

location.reload() in browser mode actually reloads the iframe, crashing test execution. Use never-resolving fetch promises for success paths that call reload.

All tests consolidated in srv/static/modules/browser-unit.browser.test.js. Config: vitest.browser-unit.config.mjs. Make target: browser-unit-test.
## feedreader-7dq.37 + feedreader-7dq.38: Migrate article-actions and articles to browser mode
Key constraint: vi.mock() causes process hangs in Vitest browser mode v4.0.18, so only tests that DON'T need mocked dependencies can migrate. Tests asserting on IntersectionObserver creation/observation and window.scrollTo calls were good candidates — they only need real browser APIs + fetch spy. Tests asserting on mocked setPaginationState, updateCounts, etc. must stay in happy-dom.

Pattern: the browser tests verify real API instance types (toBeInstanceOf(IntersectionObserver)) which is impossible in happy-dom. The articles.test.js still needs MockIntersectionObserver in its beforeEach for the remaining renderArticles tests that call initAutoMarkRead internally but don't assert on IO behavior."}

## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js

{"id":"feedreader-e2d","title":"Feed ingestion and parsing","description":"Improvements to RSS/Atom feed fetching, parsing, and scraper reliability. Covers error handling, retry logic, feed autodiscovery, content extraction quality, conditional GET edge cases, and scraper robustness.","acceptance_criteria":"Feeds are fetched reliably with clear error reporting, parsers handle edge cases gracefully, and scrapers recover from transient failures.","notes":"## feedreader-e2d.3.5: Feed source registry
- Created srv/sources/ package with Source interface and Registry
- Interface: Match(url, feedType) bool, NormalizeURL(ctx, url), ResolveName(ctx, url, scraperConfig), FeedType()
- Registry.Resolve() does full normalization+naming in one call; returns inputs unchanged if no source matches
- Steam/Reddit/HuggingFace sources migrated with full test coverage
- Server.Sources field initialized via sources.DefaultRegistry() in New() and test helpers
- New sources just need to implement the interface and register in defaults.go
- Tests that used internal steamClient swapping now use sources.SetSteamClient() or package-internal access
## feedreader-e2d.4: GitHub Releases feed type\n- Simple source: no HTTP calls needed (no API for name resolution, just regex)\n- Matches on feedType='github' rather than URL pattern — client sends the type\n- NormalizeURL handles both owner/repo shorthand and full GitHub URLs including .git suffix\n- FeedType() returns 'rss' to route through standard Atom parser\n- GitHub release Atom feeds work perfectly with existing parser — entries have title, HTML content, author, timestamps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:54:01Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:02:30Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T09:02:30Z","close_reason":"all steps complete"}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3","title":"YouTube Playlist Feed Support","description":"Support YouTube playlists as feed sources. YouTube exposes playlist Atom feeds at `https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?playlist_id=PLxxx` but they are limited to 15 entries in playlist-position order. This works for 'add to top' playlists (newest first) but breaks for 'add to bottom' playlists where new items append past position 15 and never appear in the feed. Phase 1 uses the existing RSS/Atom parser with URL auto-detection. Phase 2 adds YouTube Data API v3 integration to support 'add to bottom' playlists via paginated playlistItems.list calls.","design":"Two-phase approach:

**Phase 1 — RSS-only (no API key needed):**
- Auto-convert YouTube URLs to feed URLs in apiCreateFeed: `youtube.com/playlist?list=PLxxx` → `youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?playlist_id=PLxxx`, and channel URLs → channel feed URLs
- Auto-name feeds from the Atom feed title
- Existing Atom parser already handles YouTube entries (media:group, embeds, thumbnails, yt:video GUIDs)
- This supports 'add to top' playlists immediately

**Phase 2 — YouTube Data API for 'add to bottom' playlists:**
- New user-level setting for YouTube API key (stored in user_settings)
- New feed type `youtube-playlist` that fetches via Data API v3 playlistItems.list
- Supports pagination to reach the last page of a playlist and find newly appended videos
- Track last-seen playlist position or item count to efficiently detect new additions
- Convert API responses to FeedItem structs matching the existing pipeline
- Falls back to RSS if no API key is configured

Articles from both phases are stored and displayed as normal feed items — no playlist-specific UX.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Pasting a YouTube playlist URL auto-converts to feed URL and fetches successfully
2. Pasting a YouTube channel URL auto-converts to feed URL and fetches successfully
3. Feed auto-names from the YouTube feed title
4. 'Add to top' playlists show new videos via standard RSS polling
5. 'Add to bottom' playlists show new videos via YouTube Data API when API key is configured
6. All YouTube articles display with embedded video player and thumbnail
7. Retention manager does not prematurely delete playlist articles","notes":"## feedreader-e2d.3.1: YouTube feed type in UI and server
- YouTubeSource registered in sources/defaults.go
- @handle resolution works by scraping the YouTube page HTML for canonical link or channelId meta tag
- FeedType() returns 'rss' — no new DB feed type needed since YouTube Atom feeds work with existing parser
- Client sends raw YouTube URL with feedType='youtube'; all normalization is server-side
- ParseYouTubeURL() exported for potential use by other components
- YouTube Atom feed title extraction uses a lightweight XML decode (64KB limit)
## feedreader-e2d.3.2: YouTube Data API integration\n- Package at srv/youtube/ with Client struct, NewClient(apiKey)\n- GUIDs use 'yt:video:' prefix matching existing YouTube Atom parser convention\n- Content includes iframe embed + HTML-escaped description paragraph\n- Thumbnail selection prefers high > medium > default\n- API base URL is a const (apiBaseURL) for easy testing\n- Tests use a roundTripFunc transport to redirect requests to httptest.Server\n- maxPages=0 means unlimited; useful for the 'add to bottom' task to control how deep we paginate
## feedreader-e2d.3.3: 'Add to bottom' playlist support
- youtube.Item type replaces feeds.FeedItem in the youtube package to avoid import cycle (youtube → feeds → youtube)
- YouTubePlaylistConfig struct lives in feeds package (exported for use by server.go)
- Position tracking uses last_known_count vs totalResults from API; items past last_known_count are new
- Fetcher reads youtubeApiKey from user_settings via GetUserSetting query
- New UpdateFeedScraperConfig query for persisting config changes without touching other feed fields
- playlistOrder 'bottom' triggers youtube-playlist type; 'top' or empty stays RSS
- Playlist detection in UI uses regex /\/playlist\?list=|[?&]playlist_id=/ on the input field
## feedreader-e2d.3.4: Retention exemption for YouTube playlist feeds
- Chose option 2 (per-feed skip_retention boolean) — general-purpose, not YouTube-specific
- Column defaults to 0; auto-set to 1 for youtube-playlist feed type on creation
- All 4 retention queries updated (Delete/Count, per-user and global variants)
- Exposed in edit feed modal so users can toggle it for any feed
- No separate DB query needed — piggybacked on existing CreateFeed and UpdateFeed queries","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:13:37Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:02:30Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T09:02:30Z","close_reason":"All children completed: YouTube feed type with URL auto-detection, Data API integration, 'add to bottom' playlist support with position tracking, and retention exemption for playlist feeds."}

**Templates (feeds.html):**

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.2","title":"YouTube Data API integration for playlist fetching","description":"Add a YouTube Data API v3 client for fetching playlist items. This is needed because the RSS feed only returns the first 15 items in playlist order, which misses new additions to 'add to bottom' playlists.

Implementation:
- New package `srv/youtube/` with a client that calls `playlistItems.list` endpoint
- Accepts an API key (no OAuth needed — public playlist data only)
- Supports pagination via pageToken to traverse the full playlist
- Returns items as FeedItem structs compatible with the existing pipeline
- Extract video title, description, thumbnail, published date, channel/author, and video URL
- Handle API errors gracefully (quota exceeded, invalid key, playlist not found)

The YouTube Data API v3 playlistItems.list endpoint:
- `GET https://www.googleapis.com/youtube/v3/playlistItems?part=snippet&playlistId=PLxxx&maxResults=50&pageToken=xxx`
- Returns up to 50 items per page with nextPageToken for pagination
- snippet includes: title, description, thumbnails, videoOwnerChannelTitle, publishedAt, resourceId.videoId","acceptance_criteria":"1. YouTube API client can fetch all items from a playlist via pagination
2. API responses are converted to FeedItem structs with correct GUID, title, URL, thumbnail, content (embed), and published date
3. API errors (quota, invalid key, not found) produce clear error messages
4. Client is tested with mock responses","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"exe.dev user","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:13:58Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T03:12:43Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T03:12:43Z","close_reason":"Created srv/youtube/ package with YouTube Data API v3 client. {"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.3","title":"'Add to bottom' playlist support with position tracking","description":"Wire the YouTube Data API client into the feed fetcher to support playlists where new videos are appended to the end (past position 15, invisible to RSS).

Implementation:
- When a YouTube playlist feed is created with 'add to bottom' order, store as feed_type `youtube-playlist` instead of `rss`
- Add `youtube-playlist` case to the fetcher switch statement
- Store playlist config in scraper_config JSON: `{"playlist_id": "PLxxx"}`
- On each fetch, use the Data API to paginate to the end of the playlist and find newly added items
- Track `last_known_count` in scraper_config to avoid re-fetching everything each poll
- Convert API responses to FeedItem and store via CreateArticle upsert (yt:video GUID prevents duplicates)
- If no YouTube API key is configured, fall back to RSS (first 15 items only) and log a warning

**UI changes:**
- Add 'add to top' / 'add to bottom' option in the YouTube feed type form
- 'Add to top' uses plain RSS (feed_type: rss) — works today
- 'Add to bottom' uses Data API (feed_type: youtube-playlist) — requires API key

**User-level API key:**
- Add a `youtube_api_key` user setting (stored in user_settings table)
- Expose in the settings page
- Pass to the fetcher when constructing the YouTube API client","acceptance_criteria":"1. Feed type youtube-playlist fetches new items appended to the end of a playlist
2. Only newly added items are fetched on subsequent polls (not the full playlist)
3. Duplicate videos are handled via GUID upsert
4. Works with user-configured YouTube API key from settings
5. Falls back to RSS gracefully when no API key is set
6. Position tracking persists across fetches","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"exe.dev user","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:10Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:10:16Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T04:10:16Z","close_reason":"Implemented youtube-playlist feed type with Data API integration. UI adds playlist order selector and YouTube API key setting. Refactored youtube.Item to break import cycle. All checks pass."}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.4","title":"Retention exemption for YouTube playlist feeds","description":"The retention manager cleans up articles older than 30 days. For YouTube playlist feeds, this is problematic — a user may add a playlist of old videos they intend to watch over time. The published dates will be far in the past and articles could be cleaned up before the user sees them.

Options (pick one during implementation):
1. Exempt youtube-playlist feed type from retention entirely
2. Add a per-feed 'skip retention' flag  
3. Only retain based on fetched_at (not published_at) for playlist feeds

The simplest approach is option 2 — a per-feed boolean that defaults to true for youtube-playlist feeds. This is also useful for other feed types where users want to keep old articles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Articles from YouTube playlist feeds are not deleted by the retention manager
2. The exemption mechanism is general-purpose (not YouTube-specific) so other feeds can opt in","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"exe.dev user","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:14:19Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T05:15:35Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T05:15:35Z","close_reason":"Added skip_retention column to feeds table (migration 017). Retention queries (Delete/Count, per-user and global) now exclude feeds with skip_retention=1. YouTube-playlist feeds auto-set skip_retention=1 on creation. The flag is exposed in the feed edit modal as a 'Skip retention cleanup' checkbox, allowing any feed to opt out of retention. Updated sqlc queries, server handlers, JS modal, and all related tests."}

{"id":"feedreader-e2d.3.5","title":"Refactor feed source handling into a registry pattern","description":"Replace the if-then chain in apiCreateFeed with a feed source registry. Currently, URL normalization and auto-naming are scattered across inline if-blocks for Steam, Reddit, and HuggingFace. Adding YouTube would extend this further.

Extract a FeedSource registry where each source declares:
- `Match(url, feedType string) bool` — does this source handle this input?
- `NormalizeURL(ctx, url string) (string, error)` — convert human URL to feed URL (e.g. Steam news → RSS)
- `ResolveName(ctx, url, config string) string` — derive a feed name
- `FeedType() string` — what feed_type to store (e.g. Reddit UI type → "rss" on the wire)

The apiCreateFeed handler iterates the registry instead of branching. Each source is independently testable.

Sources to migrate:
- Steam: NormalizeURL (news→feed conversion) + ResolveName (Steam API name lookup)
- Reddit: ResolveName (regex extract r/name). Note: Reddit URL construction happens client-side, server only auto-names.
- HuggingFace: ResolveName (API call). Note: config-based, feedType stays "huggingface".

This sets up a clean pattern for YouTube and future sources.","acceptance_criteria":"1. apiCreateFeed no longer has feed-source-specific if-blocks — uses registry loop instead
2. Steam, Reddit, and HuggingFace behavior is unchanged
3. Each source is unit-testable in isolation
4. Adding a new source requires only registering a new entry","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"exe.dev user","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:32:56Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T02:11:29Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T02:11:29Z","close_reason":"Extracted feed source logic into srv/sources/ package with Registry pattern. Steam, Reddit, and HuggingFace sources implement the Source interface (Match, NormalizeURL, ResolveName, FeedType). apiCreateFeed now calls s.Sources.Resolve() instead of inline if-blocks. All tests migrated and passing. Adding a new source requires only implementing the interface and registering in defaults.go."}

{"id":"feedreader-7dq.35.6","title":"Replace networkidle with domcontentloaded in layout test commands","description":"The `measureLayout` and `fetchPageHTML` custom commands in `vitest.browser.config.mjs` use `waitUntil: 'networkidle'`, which waits for zero network requests over a 500ms window. This adds at least 500ms per test and accounts for ~85% of each call's cost (~830ms of ~960ms).

Layout measurements only need the DOM and CSS to be ready — they don't depend on async API calls settling. Switching to `domcontentloaded` (or `load`) should be sufficient and cut per-test time significantly.

**Files:** `vitest.browser.config.mjs` — update the `waitUntil` option in `fetchPageHTML`, `measureLayout`, and `measureLayoutMultiWidth` commands.

**Risk:** If the app's CSS is loaded asynchronously or layout depends on JS that runs after DOMContentLoaded, measurements could be taken too early. Verify by comparing bounding rects before and after the change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 30 layout tests still pass
2. No flaky failures from measuring before layout settles
3. Measurable reduction in total test runtime","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:57:48Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:57:48Z"}

new_value: Moved let _actionListenerAC, _articleListListenerAC, and _paginationAC declarations above their respective reset functions in article-actions.js, articles.js, and pagination.js. All 8 ESLint no-use-before-define warnings resolved. make check passes clean.

Replaced waitUntil: 'networkidle' with waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' in all 3 Playwright page.goto() calls in vitest.browser.config.mjs (fetchPageHTML, measureLayout, measureLayoutMultiWidth). All 30 layout tests pass. No flaky failures observed.

{"notes":"## Environment note\nThe JS test environment is **happy-dom** (configured in vitest.config.mjs), not jsdom. happy-dom normalizes self-closing SVG/HTML tags (e.g. `<path .../>` → `<path ...></path>`), so innerHTML assertions should use `toContain` with distinguishing substrings rather than exact string matching against source constants.
## feedreader-7dq.21: Audit timestamps.test.js
Module is trivially simple (6 lines). Tests were already solid. Added invalid date edge case. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.22: Audit toast.test.js
Found and fixed 5 weaknesses directly (no sub-epic). Key issue: the dismiss button test was testing nothing — it clicked a button, switched to fake timers, then 'clicked again on a second toast' per comment but never created one, with zero assertions. The getContainer() recreation path (container removed from DOM) was untested despite having explicit logic for it.

Also, initAlertsPage and initAlertDetailPage had untested delegated click handlers. Tests went from 30 to 45. The alerts module has good separation of concerns making it straightforward to test.
## feedreader-7dq.9: Audit icons.test.js
Pure constants module, tests were already solid. No changes needed.

## feedreader-7dq.23: Audit utils.test.js
Added 15 tests (22→37), no sub-epic needed. Main weakness pattern: missing boundary tests (exact-length thresholds in formatTimeAgo and truncateText) and missing edge case inputs (invalid dates, empty strings, non-string types). Note: happy-dom does not suppress script tag content via innerHTML like real browsers, so stripHtml script-stripping test was replaced with plain text test.
## feedreader-7dq.2: Audit api.test.js
Small module (26 lines) with 5 clear branches. Tests were decent but missed: JSON response without error field, empty error string (both fall back to 'Request failed'), network errors from fetch rejection, and explicit null data. Added 5 tests, no sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.6: Audit dropdown.test.js
Existing tests had good structural coverage but missed all keyboard activation paths (Enter/Space), several no-op edge cases (Escape with no open dropdown, Enter on non-menu-item), and the toggleDropdown first-item-focus behavior. Also the first describe block used .dropdown-btn class instead of .dropdown-toggle which meant those tests didn't exercise aria-expanded paths. Added 10 tests, no sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.10: Audit modal.test.js
Found 9 gaps, all fixed directly. Key patterns: untested early-return guards (null modal), untested default behavior when optional params omitted (focusEl), untested no-op paths (closeModal with no active modal, trapFocus with no focusable elements), and missing boundary condition coverage for trapFocus (Tab when not at last, Shift+Tab when not at first). The modal-content fallback in trapFocus was also untested.

## feedreader-7dq.15: Audit read-button.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Existing tests verified title and innerHTML but missed aria-label. Also added toggle back-and-forth and truthy/falsy coercion tests. Module is very simple (16 lines of logic) so test coverage is now comprehensive.
## feedreader-7dq.11: Audit offline.test.js
Found 13 gaps, all fixed directly. Key constraint: _isStandalone is a module-level constant evaluated at import time, always false in happy-dom. This means handleOnlineStateChange, initOfflineSupport, and updateQueueCacheIfStandalone can only be tested for their early-return path. Main weakness pattern: replayPendingActions had many untested branches (non-matching messages, null data, empty actions, missing actions field, unknown action types, API failures). Also added round-trip integration test for disableNonQueueUI → enableAllUI.

## feedreader-7dq.12: Audit opml.test.js
Found 5 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness: success test didn't verify location.reload, headers, or FormData content. Error tests didn't verify reload was NOT called. Missing branches: no error field fallback, non-JSON response. Also added delegation tests (nested child click, negative guard). Found and fixed mock state leakage issue — needed vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach for module-level mocks.
## feedreader-7dq.25: Refactor _isStandalone
Key insight: vi.spyOn(module, 'fn').mockReturnValue() does NOT work for ESM internal calls — the internal code still calls the original function, not the spy. The correct approach is to mock the underlying browser API (window.matchMedia) that the function calls. Created a mockStandaloneMode() helper that mocks matchMedia to return {matches: true}.

## feedreader-7dq.3: Audit articles.test.js
Found 37 gaps, all fixed directly without a sub-epic. Main weakness patterns: (1) buildArticleCardHtml had many untested ternary branches (no-url, author, summary/content combos, image placeholder, sort-time fallback), (2) showHiddenArticles error path (showToast) was untested, (3) renderArticles didn't verify aria-busy, pagination state, or empty-list button visibility, (4) processEmbeds had 4 untested branches. Note: toast.js needed to be added as a vi.mock for error path testing.
## feedreader-7dq.4: Audit counts.test.js
Added 11 tests (14→25). Found mock state leakage: vi.restoreAllMocks() doesn't clear call counts on vi.fn() from vi.mock() — need vi.clearAllMocks() too. Key insight: the test file was missing a toast.js mock entirely, so the showToast error path was invisible. The alerts badge was completely untested. All aria-label attributes had zero coverage despite being accessibility-critical.

## feedreader-7dq.14: Audit queue.test.js
Added 4 tests (8→12). Module is tiny (39 lines). Notable: queueNext has no try/catch so API errors propagate — tested this explicitly. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.5: Audit drag-drop.test.js
Major gap: api.js and toast.js were not mocked at all, so the entire drop handler (both reorder and nesting paths) was untested. This is the most complex drag-and-drop module with async API calls, shift-key nesting, placeholder positioning, and cross-container sync. All branches now covered. The vi.waitFor() pattern works well for asserting on async drop handlers.

## feedreader-7dq.7: Audit feed-errors.test.js
Small module with good existing coverage. Main gap was no XSS escaping test despite escapeHtml being called. The banner-exists-outside-articles-view path was also untested. No complexity warranting a sub-epic.
## feedreader-7dq.16: Audit scraper-page.test.js
Major gap pattern: private functions called via initScraperPage (checkAiStatus, initAiForm, initManualForm) were completely untested — they have complex async flows with success/error/finally paths. modal.js was unmocked so openModal/closeModal calls were invisible. The initScraperPageListeners describe block uses a pattern of calling initScraperPageListeners() once at the describe level since listeners accumulate on document — this works well but means delegation tests share state.

## feedreader-7dq.17: Audit search.test.js
toast.js was imported by the source but not mocked in tests, so all error paths that call showToast were invisible. The setShowingHiddenArticles mock was imported in the mock declaration but never imported in test code for assertions. Key pattern: _resetSearchState calls setShowingHiddenArticles(false) so tests that check it wasn't called need vi.clearAllMocks() after beforeEach.
## feedreader-7dq.24: Audit views.test.js
Added 7 tests. Main gap: initView was completely untested, and migrateLegacyViewDefaults only tested the localMap loop, not the direct keys loop. Module is clean with good separation. No sub-epic needed.

## feedreader-7dq.20: Audit sidebar.test.js
Added 5 tests. Notable: the navigate-folder non-articles-page test in initSidebarListeners had zero assertions — just comments saying what should happen. The toggleFolderCollapse guard (if (catId)) for falsy values was untested. navigateFolder's null callback and missing folder-name fallback were both uncovered. No sub-epic needed.
## feedreader-7dq.8: Audit folders.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Main gap: initCategorySettingsPage was entirely untested (3 tests: success, error, no-ops). The delegation handler for unparent-category and delete-exclusion had zero coverage despite being registered in initFoldersPageListeners. openModal/closeModal from modal.js were never mocked so their calls were invisible. All success paths lacked location.reload() verification, meaning tests passed even if the reload never happened. Pattern: vi.mock('./modal.js') at module level is needed to verify modal lifecycle.

## feedreader-7dq.13: Audit pagination.test.js
Found 15 gaps, all fixed directly. Most critical: the entire error path (catch block with console.error + showToast) had zero coverage. The done-state transitions (empty articles → done, partial page → done, full page → not done) were unverified despite being core pagination logic. The include_read=1 param tied to getShowingHiddenArticles() was untested. Gotcha: loadMoreArticles has a finally block that calls checkScrollForMore(), which can recursively trigger another loadMoreArticles if the scroll position is near the bottom. Tests that don't want cascading loads must set document.body.offsetHeight to a large value.
## feedreader-7dq.18: Audit settings-page.test.js
Found 11 gaps, all fixed directly. Main weakness patterns: all default/fallback paths in initSettingsPage were untested (the || 'show', || 'card' fallbacks), runCleanup intermediate loading state was not verified, copyNewsletterAddress checkmark icon swap and null-button guard were untested. The loadNewsletterAddress call from initSettingsPage was not verified at all.

## feedreader-7dq.19: Audit settings.test.js
Found 7 gaps, all fixed directly. Key issue: toast.js was entirely unmocked so saveSetting's catch handler calling showToast was invisible to tests. Same pattern as counts.test.js — module-level vi.mock() needed plus vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach. Also added boundary tests for getSetting (empty string vs undefined) and edge cases for applyHideEmptyFeeds (empty/NaN badge text).
## feedreader-7dq.26: Over-mocking audit
Cross-cutting findings from auditing all 27 test files (792 tests total):

**Duplication hotspots**: toast.js (13 identical mocks), api.js (7 identical mocks), and ~100 inline fetch response shapes. These are the highest-value targets for shared helpers.

**Three fetch strategies coexist**: vi.mock(api.js) vs vi.spyOn(globalThis,fetch) vs window.fetch=vi.fn(). The window.fetch assignment is worst (no auto-restore). Should standardize.

**Stateful mock anti-pattern**: settings-page.test.js reimplements settings.js internals. Since settings.js is pure localStorage logic, the real module should be used.

**Mock-heavy orchestrator tests**: spa-nav.test.js needs 6 mocks for 4 tests, suggesting it should either use fewer mocks (more integration-style) or the module should be refactored.

**Go tests are clean**: httptest.NewServer pattern, minimal mocking. No action needed.

Created 5 follow-up tasks (.27-.31) for actionable improvements.
## feedreader-7dq.27 + feedreader-7dq.30: Shared mocks and real settings
Key pattern: Vitest __mocks__/ auto-mocking works well for modules with a simple, stable API surface. Place files in __mocks__/module.js alongside the real modules, then test files just call vi.mock("./module.js") without a factory.

For settings-page.test.js, using importOriginal to get real getSetting while mocking saveSetting was the right split — getSetting is pure (reads window.__settings), saveSetting has side effects (fetch). Tests became simpler and more realistic.

Gotcha: __mocks__/ files need `import { vi } from "vitest"` at the top since they are loaded by the test runner.
## feedreader-7dq.28: Standardize fetch mocking
The three incompatible fetch mocking strategies (vi.mock api.js, vi.spyOn globalThis.fetch, window.fetch assignment) are now reduced to two: vi.mock for modules using api(), vi.spyOn for direct fetch callers. window.fetch = vi.fn() is eliminated. vi.stubGlobal('fetch') is also eliminated. Convention documented in srv/static/modules/TESTING.md.

## feedreader-7dq.32: Replace vi.waitFor polling in scraper-page.test.js
flushPromises() (setTimeout-based macrotask flush) is a reliable replacement for vi.waitFor() when all async operations use mocked functions that resolve immediately. Single flush per async step is sufficient. Runtime went from 756ms → 91ms.
## feedreader-7dq.33: Reduce pagination.test.js runtime
Key bottleneck was the PAGE_SIZE test rendering 50 full articles via buildArticleCardHtml + processEmbeds in happy-dom (~300ms). Mocking buildArticleCardHtml to return minimal HTML dropped it to 3ms. The other bottleneck (real 50ms setTimeout) was fixed with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(0). Total: 411ms → 61ms. Approach: mock articles.js via auto-mock at module level, configure buildArticleCardHtml default in beforeEach to return HTML with article-card and article-content-preview classes, then override to minimal HTML in the PAGE_SIZE test.

## feedreader-7dq.34: Consolidate remaining inline vi.mock factories
Created 6 new auto-mocks. Key insight: ESM destructured imports (import { fn } from './mod.js') in source code DO get intercepted by vi.mock() because Vitest makes the module namespace mutable — the import binding is a live reference to the mock. So auto-mocks work correctly for intercepting calls in dependent modules. Two justified inline factories remain: settings-page.test.js (needs importOriginal for real getSetting), views.test.js (stateful store mock). The views.test.js mock could potentially be replaced by using real settings.js with localStorage (same approach as settings-page task .30), but was out of scope.
## feedreader-7dq.29: API response fixture factories
Created 3 factories in test-helpers.js: makeFetchResponse(data, {ok, status}), makeCountsResponse(overrides), makeArticle(overrides). The makeFetchResponse includes both json() and text() methods on the fake Response, which is important since some error paths call .text() instead of .json(). For counts.test.js, a local mockCountsFetch() wrapper that composes both factories eliminated all 13 inline response shapes with a single function call per test.

## feedreader-7dq.31: spa-nav mock reduction
Option A (more integrative tests) was the right call. The module is a genuine orchestrator — it *should* call 6 different modules during navigation. Reducing mocks would mean testing DOM manipulation of real sidebar/views/counts modules, which adds coupling without real benefit. Instead, making each mock earn its assertions revealed that the test file was missing coverage for: popstate handling (reload on non-article pages, restore unread/starred), error handling (both navigateToNavRoute and restoreFromState catch blocks), route filtering (feed/category clicks correctly ignored), and DOM side effects (dropdown clearing, feed-action button hiding). The restoreFromState feed/category paths use dynamic import('./feeds.js') which is harder to test in isolation — would need vi.mock with dynamic import support.
## feedreader-7dq.39 + feedreader-7dq.40: Browser mode migration for drag-drop and pagination
Three critical limitations of Vitest browser mode v4.0.18 discovered:

1. **vi.mock() causes process hangs** — Module mocking in browser mode prevents clean process exit. Workaround: mock at fetch level with vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch').

2. **vi.spyOn on ESM namespaces fails** — 'Cannot spy on export — Module namespace is not configurable in ESM'. Cannot intercept module-internal calls. Workaround: mock lower-level APIs (fetch, console) instead.

3. **Multi-file test execution hangs** — Running multiple browser test files causes process to never exit. Workaround: combine all browser unit tests into a single file.

Additional pattern: showToast() creates real 4-second setTimeout timers in browser mode. Track timer IDs in beforeEach and clearTimeout in afterEach to prevent timers from keeping the process alive.

location.reload() in browser mode actually reloads the iframe, crashing test execution. Use never-resolving fetch promises for success paths that call reload.

All tests consolidated in srv/static/modules/browser-unit.browser.test.js. Config: vitest.browser-unit.config.mjs. Make target: browser-unit-test.
## feedreader-7dq.37 + feedreader-7dq.38: Migrate article-actions and articles to browser mode
Key constraint: vi.mock() causes process hangs in Vitest browser mode v4.0.18, so only tests that DON'T need mocked dependencies can migrate. Tests asserting on IntersectionObserver creation/observation and window.scrollTo calls were good candidates — they only need real browser APIs + fetch spy. Tests asserting on mocked setPaginationState, updateCounts, etc. must stay in happy-dom.

Pattern: the browser tests verify real API instance types (toBeInstanceOf(IntersectionObserver)) which is impossible in happy-dom. The articles.test.js still needs MockIntersectionObserver in its beforeEach for the remaining renderArticles tests that call initAutoMarkRead internally but don't assert on IO behavior.
## feedreader-7dq.46: Lint test files with ESLint
ESLint config uses three config blocks: production files, unit test files (*.test.js), and browser test files (*.browser.test.js + tests/layout/*.test.js). Each block has appropriate globals. The old approach of a global ignores block has been replaced with file-scoped config blocks with explicit ignores. The task description originally listed 16 errors but those were preemptively fixed by adding browser globals (File, AbortSignal, DOMException, PopStateEvent, Window) in the config task — only 6 unused-var warnings remained for the fix task."}

Created 3 factories in test-helpers.js: makeFetchResponse(data, {ok, status}), makeCountsResponse(overrides), makeArticle(overrides). The makeFetchResponse includes both json() and text() methods on the fake Response, which is important since some error paths call .text() instead of .json(). For counts.test.js, a local mockCountsFetch() wrapper that composes both factories eliminated all 13 inline response shapes with a single function call per test.

## feedreader-7dq.31: spa-nav mock reduction
Option A (more integrative tests) was the right call. The module is a genuine orchestrator — it *should* call 6 different modules during navigation. Reducing mocks would mean testing DOM manipulation of real sidebar/views/counts modules, which adds coupling without real benefit. Instead, making each mock earn its assertions revealed that the test file was missing coverage for: popstate handling (reload on non-article pages, restore unread/starred), error handling (both navigateToNavRoute and restoreFromState catch blocks), route filtering (feed/category clicks correctly ignored), and DOM side effects (dropdown clearing, feed-action button hiding). The restoreFromState feed/category paths use dynamic import('./feeds.js') which is harder to test in isolation — would need vi.mock with dynamic import support.
## feedreader-7dq.39 + feedreader-7dq.40: Browser mode migration for drag-drop and pagination
Three critical limitations of Vitest browser mode v4.0.18 discovered:

1. **vi.mock() causes process hangs** — Module mocking in browser mode prevents clean process exit. Workaround: mock at fetch level with vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch').

2. **vi.spyOn on ESM namespaces fails** — 'Cannot spy on export — Module namespace is not configurable in ESM'. Cannot intercept module-internal calls. Workaround: mock lower-level APIs (fetch, console) instead.

3. **Multi-file test execution hangs** — Running multiple browser test files causes process to never exit. Workaround: combine all browser unit tests into a single file.

Additional pattern: showToast() creates real 4-second setTimeout timers in browser mode. Track timer IDs in beforeEach and clearTimeout in afterEach to prevent timers from keeping the process alive.

location.reload() in browser mode actually reloads the iframe, crashing test execution. Use never-resolving fetch promises for success paths that call reload.

All tests consolidated in srv/static/modules/browser-unit.browser.test.js. Config: vitest.browser-unit.config.mjs. Make target: browser-unit-test.
## feedreader-7dq.37 + feedreader-7dq.38: Migrate article-actions and articles to browser mode
Key constraint: vi.mock() causes process hangs in Vitest browser mode v4.0.18, so only tests that DON'T need mocked dependencies can migrate. Tests asserting on IntersectionObserver creation/observation and window.scrollTo calls were good candidates — they only need real browser APIs + fetch spy. Tests asserting on mocked setPaginationState, updateCounts, etc. must stay in happy-dom.

Pattern: the browser tests verify real API instance types (toBeInstanceOf(IntersectionObserver)) which is impossible in happy-dom. This wastes 10ms of real time per call when a zero-delay flush would suffice (all I/O is mocked).

**Affected files:**
- folders.test.js:425 — 1 call
- opml.test.js:201, 214 — 2 calls
- settings-page.test.js:511, 527, 545 — 3 calls
- settings.test.js:81 — 1 call
- articles.test.js:694 — 1 call (also tracked in feedreader-7dq.43)

Total: 8 calls wasting 10ms each = ~80ms across the test suite.

**Fix:** Replace `setTimeout(r, 10)` with `setTimeout(r, 0)` (or `queueMicrotask`). Per STYLE.md, real-time sleeps in tests are prohibited when all I/O is mocked.

Note: browser-unit.browser.test.js:366 has a 200ms sleep that may be legitimate (real browser layout settling) — investigate but don't blindly change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. No `setTimeout(r, N)` calls with N > 0 remain in unit test files (excluding browser tests with justification)
