{"id":"feedreader-lc7.2.2","title":"Cloudflare Tunnel + Access auth provider","description":"Add a Cloudflare Access auth provider.

Headers:
- Cf-Access-Authenticated-User-Email — user email (used as identity)
- Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion — JWT token

Cloudflare Access doesn't provide a stable user ID separate from email, so email is the identifier. JWT validation against the Cloudflare certs endpoint (https://<team>.cloudflareaccess.com/cdn-cgi/access/certs) should be supported to prevent header spoofing.

Config: team domain (for JWT validation), optional audience tag.","notes":"Requirement: include new/updated tests for all changed code. make check must pass before closing.
Decision: use github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5 for Cloudflare Access JWT validation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"exedev@lynx-fairy.exe.xyz","created_at":"2026-03-06T02:53:36Z","created_by":"exe.dev user","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:15:48Z"}

new_value: Added CloudflareAccessProvider in srv/auth_cloudflare.go with JWT validation against Cloudflare JWKS endpoint. Uses golang-jwt/jwt/v5 for ES256 JWT parsing. Validates issuer (team domain), optional audience, and signature. JWKS keys cached 10min. Email extracted from validated JWT (not header). HTTPClient interface for testability. 18 tests in auth_cloudflare_test.go. All make check passes.

{"notes":"## feedreader-lc7.1: Remove Shelley scraper generator
Completed clean removal of all Shelley AI scraper code. 852 lines deleted across 13 files. Also removed AI-specific rate limiting from ratelimit.go since there are no AI endpoints left. The scraper page now shows only the manual config section (no tabs). All tests pass.
## feedreader-lc7.2.1: Refactor auth middleware to be provider-pluggable
Implemented AuthProvider interface in srv/auth.go. Three concrete providers: ExeDevProvider (backward compat), ProxyHeaderProvider (configurable headers for any reverse proxy), DevProvider (DEV=1 bypass). Server.AuthProvider field defaults to ExeDevProvider. Unauthenticated pages now return 401 HTML instead of redirecting to /__exe.dev/login. The DEV=1 fallback is preserved as a secondary check after the primary provider returns nil. The config system (lc7.4) was recorded as a decision but not yet implemented in code — provider selection is currently done by setting Server.AuthProvider directly. The config file integration will come with lc7.12 (TOML library) or a separate implementation task.
## feedreader-lc7.2.3: Tailscale Serve/Funnel auth provider
Added TailscaleProvider in srv/auth_tailscale.go. Uses Tailscale-User-Login as both ExternalID and Email since Tailscale doesn't provide a separate stable user ID. Exported header constants for all three Tailscale headers (Login, Name, Profile-Pic) for use by config system later. No JWT validation — Tailscale authenticates at the transport layer. Pattern follows the same simple style as ExeDevProvider (value receiver, no config fields).
## feedreader-lc7.2.5: Authelia auth provider\nAdded AutheliaProvider following the same value-receiver pattern as TailscaleProvider. Remote-User is the stable identifier, Remote-Email provides email. Note: Authelia uses the same default header names as ProxyHeaderProvider (Remote-User, Remote-Email), so users could also just use ProxyHeaderProvider. The dedicated AutheliaProvider exists for clarity in config and to export the Authelia-specific Remote-Name and Remote-Groups header constants for potential future use (e.g., group-based access control).
## feedreader-lc7.2.8: CLI init command for config generation
Added 'feedreader init' subcommand and new config package. Key decisions:
- Config struct lives in config/config.go, separate from srv/ to avoid import cycles
- Uses pelletier/go-toml/v2 per lc7.12 decision
- promptWriter helper wraps io.Writer for errcheck-clean interactive output
- Proxy config section is only emitted when non-default headers are used (clean TOML output)
- Subcommand detected before flag.Parse() to avoid flag conflicts
- initCmd accepts io.Reader/io.Writer for testability (no os.Stdin/Stdout dependency)
- The config package is now ready for use by the setup UI (lc7.2.6) and future --config flag integration in main.go
## feedreader-lc7.2.4: OAuth2 Proxy auth provider
Added OAuth2ProxyProvider following the same value-receiver pattern as Tailscale/Authelia. Uses X-Forwarded-Email as primary identifier (external ID), with X-Forwarded-User as fallback when email is absent. Exports header constants for all four OAuth2 Proxy headers (User, Email, Preferred-Username, Groups) for future config system use. Note: OAuth2 Proxy's default headers (X-Forwarded-User/Email) overlap with ProxyHeaderProvider when custom headers are configured, but the dedicated provider gives users a clear, named option in config.
## feedreader-lc7.2.2: Cloudflare Tunnel + Access auth provider
Added CloudflareAccessProvider with full JWT validation using golang-jwt/jwt/v5. Key design decisions:
- Uses ES256 (P-256 ECDSA) key parsing from Cloudflare's JWKS endpoint
- JWT email claim is authoritative (preferred over Cf-Access-Authenticated-User-Email header) to prevent header spoofing
- JWKS keys cached 10 minutes with automatic refresh on cache miss (unknown kid triggers refetch)
- HTTPClient interface (not concrete *http.Client) enables clean test injection without real HTTP calls
- Optional audience validation — when Audience is empty, aud claim is not checked
- Both email header present without JWT, and JWT present without email header, are handled correctly
- Added golang-jwt/jwt/v5 dependency to go.mod"}

Added TailscaleProvider in srv/auth_tailscale.go. Uses Tailscale-User-Login as both ExternalID and Email since Tailscale doesn't provide a separate stable user ID. {"notes":"## feedreader-lc7.1: Remove Shelley scraper generator
Completed clean removal of all Shelley AI scraper code. 852 lines deleted across 13 files. Also removed AI-specific rate limiting from ratelimit.go since there are no AI endpoints left. The scraper page now shows only the manual config section (no tabs). All tests pass.
## feedreader-lc7.2.1: Refactor auth middleware to be provider-pluggable
Implemented AuthProvider interface in srv/auth.go. Three concrete providers: ExeDevProvider (backward compat), ProxyHeaderProvider (configurable headers for any reverse proxy), DevProvider (DEV=1 bypass). Server.AuthProvider field defaults to ExeDevProvider. Unauthenticated pages now return 401 HTML instead of redirecting to /__exe.dev/login. The DEV=1 fallback is preserved as a secondary check after the primary provider returns nil. The config system (lc7.4) was recorded as a decision but not yet implemented in code — provider selection is currently done by setting Server.AuthProvider directly. The config file integration will come with lc7.12 (TOML library) or a separate implementation task.
## feedreader-lc7.2.3: Tailscale Serve/Funnel auth provider
Added TailscaleProvider in srv/auth_tailscale.go. Uses Tailscale-User-Login as both ExternalID and Email since Tailscale doesn't provide a separate stable user ID. Exported header constants for all three Tailscale headers (Login, Name, Profile-Pic) for use by config system later. No JWT validation — Tailscale authenticates at the transport layer. Pattern follows the same simple style as ExeDevProvider (value receiver, no config fields).
## feedreader-lc7.2.5: Authelia auth provider\nAdded AutheliaProvider following the same value-receiver pattern as TailscaleProvider. Remote-User is the stable identifier, Remote-Email provides email. Note: Authelia uses the same default header names as ProxyHeaderProvider (Remote-User, Remote-Email), so users could also just use ProxyHeaderProvider. The dedicated AutheliaProvider exists for clarity in config and to export the Authelia-specific Remote-Name and Remote-Groups header constants for potential future use (e.g., group-based access control).
## feedreader-lc7.2.8: CLI init command for config generation
Added 'feedreader init' subcommand and new config package. Key decisions:
- Config struct lives in config/config.go, separate from srv/ to avoid import cycles
- Uses pelletier/go-toml/v2 per lc7.12 decision
- promptWriter helper wraps io.Writer for errcheck-clean interactive output
- Proxy config section is only emitted when non-default headers are used (clean TOML output)
- Subcommand detected before flag.Parse() to avoid flag conflicts
- initCmd accepts io.Reader/io.Writer for testability (no os.Stdin/Stdout dependency)
- The config package is now ready for use by the setup UI (lc7.2.6) and future --config flag integration in main.go
## feedreader-lc7.2.4: OAuth2 Proxy auth provider
Added OAuth2ProxyProvider following the same value-receiver pattern as Tailscale/Authelia. Uses X-Forwarded-Email as primary identifier (external ID), with X-Forwarded-User as fallback when email is absent. Exports header constants for all four OAuth2 Proxy headers (User, Email, Preferred-Username, Groups) for future config system use. Note: OAuth2 Proxy's default headers (X-Forwarded-User/Email) overlap with ProxyHeaderProvider when custom headers are configured, but the dedicated provider gives users a clear, named option in config.
## feedreader-lc7.2.2: Cloudflare Tunnel + Access auth provider
Added CloudflareAccessProvider with full JWT validation using golang-jwt/jwt/v5. Key design decisions:
- Uses ES256 (P-256 ECDSA) key parsing from Cloudflare's JWKS endpoint
- JWT email claim is authoritative (preferred over Cf-Access-Authenticated-User-Email header) to prevent header spoofing
- JWKS keys cached 10 minutes with automatic refresh on cache miss (unknown kid triggers refetch)
- HTTPClient interface (not concrete *http.Client) enables clean test injection without real HTTP calls
- Optional audience validation — when Audience is empty, aud claim is not checked
- Both email header present without JWT, and JWT present without email header, are handled correctly
- Added golang-jwt/jwt/v5 dependency to go.mod
## feedreader-lc7.2.6: Minimal standalone setup UI
Added SetupProvider (sentinel auth provider that always returns nil) and SetupHandler (serves self-contained setup UI). When no auth is configured, all non-static routes get the setup page. The page is a standalone HTML/CSS/JS page with no dependency on base.html or the template system. Includes provider selection with conditional config fields for proxy (custom headers), cloudflare (team domain + audience), and simple providers. Non-local access gets a warning banner. POST /setup/save validates input, writes config.toml, and fires an OnConfigSaved callback (for restart). Integration into main.go (detecting SetupProvider and wiring it up) is left to a future task — the setup UI is purely the handler + provider + page. The config package and init CLI command (lc7.2.8) are already in place."}

Multi-stage build:
1. Go builder stage — compiles the binary
2. Minimal runtime stage (alpine or distroless) — just the binary

- Expose port 8000
- Accept config via /data/config.toml (or CONFIG_FILE env var)
- Store SQLite DB at /data/db.sqlite3 (or DB path from config)
- /data as a volume mount point for persistence
- Non-root user

Example usage:
```
docker run -p 8000:8000 -v feedreader-data:/data feedreader
```
